jobs.title: Software Engineer - C, Operating systems, Data Structures
jobs.description:
Meet the Team

The Cisco Data Center India group is a pivotal force in the networking world, dedicated to architecting, designing, and delivering state-of-the-art data center solutions for the globe's most demanding customers.

This dynamic team is the proud owner and continuous innovator of NXOS software, the sophisticated operating system that powers Cisco's advanced data center switches. Their commitment extends across the entire Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), engaging from initial requirements definition with product managers through to seamless deployment, integration, and ongoing support. This end-to-end involvement ensures deep collaboration and responsiveness to a diverse spectrum of global clients.

The team's strength lies in its profound technical expertise, spanning critical areas such as SAN platforms, Optics, Fibre Channel protocols, NVMe, and Cisco Nexus OS. They are consistently at the forefront of innovation, pioneering solutions that anticipate and meet futuristic market demands.

Your Impact
• As a Software Engineer. Develop features for switches that are deployed by some of the top tier Data center customers in the world
• Part of development team, which is responsible for Feature development, testing & integration into customer environments.
• Expected to have regular interactions with SMEs in other area of development team, testing teams and customer teams to translate requirements into working solutions
• Develop expertise in Data center products and Storage/Networking protocols.
• Learn Cisco development/unit-test environments.
• Actively participate in issue triage, providing essential support, monitoring system performance, and driving timely resolutions.
• Work with a BU-wide vibrant technical community, learning from experts and translating this learning into exciting opportunities for personal growth.

Minimum Qualifications:
• Experience: 5 years to 8 years.
• Bachelor’s degree in CS/EE/EC or technical equivalent.
• Expert knowledge and hands-on experience in C
• Sound knowledge of operating system concepts and system programming on UNIX based systems
• Network and multithreaded programming

Preferred Qualifications:
• Exposure to Storage protocol
• Exposure to Fiber channel standards
• Exposure to L2/L3 protocols
• Experience working with switch/router products
• Experience with Python

Why Cisco?

At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.

Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere.

We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

jobs.title: Analytics & Intelligence Engineer
jobs.description:
About The Company

Our client is a Singapore-headquartered AI-native data & service platform delivering cyber, digital, and risk solutions across Asia. They help essential service enterprises make responsible decisions through the collective intelligence of humans and machines.

AI is embedded in how they build and run systems, but humans remain accountable.

About The Role

We’re looking for an Analytics & Intelligence Engineer to help deliver accurate, trusted insights that drive real-world outcomes. This role sits at the intersection of data, reporting, operational decision-making, and narrative clarity — ensuring stakeholders don’t just see the numbers, but understand what they mean.

This is a role for someone who cares deeply about metrics correctness, interpretation, and the quality of the story data tells.

What You’ll Own

As an Analytics & Intelligence Engineer, you will own:
• Metrics, reporting, and narrative correctness
• Interpretation and explanation of outcomes
• Clear communication of insights, including uncertainty where it exists
• Continuous improvement of reporting reliability and trust

Build Focus: Service

Your build focus is Service, meaning you contribute to domain intelligence where outcomes are defined — such as Fraud, SecOps, Asset Intelligence, or other business-critical service areas.

This focus defines your long-term ownership: the analytics capability that supports real operational decisions.

Run Focus: Present

Your run focus is Present, meaning your work directly supports how outcomes are delivered, explained, and consumed.

You ensure reporting outputs are not only accurate, but understandable and decision-ready.

Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will:
• Build and maintain reporting solutions that deliver accurate, reliable metrics
• Design, validate, and improve analytics logic that supports operational outcomes
• Investigate anomalies and inconsistencies in reporting outputs and underlying datasets
• Translate complex data into clear narratives that stakeholders can act on
• Create explanations that help teams understand why outcomes occurred, not just what happened
• Collaborate with engineers, analysts, and domain teams to ensure alignment on metric definitions and reporting intent
• Own the correctness of dashboards, reports, and insights delivered to end users
• Apply validation and quality checks before reports are released or consumed
• Document metric definitions, assumptions, and limitations to ensure transparency and long-term trust

AI-Enabled Build & Run

We use AI as a productivity accelerator — not as a decision-maker.

You Will Use AI To
• Generate and refine queries
• Draft reports, summaries, and narratives
• Detect anomalies and inconsistencies
• Explore edge cases and propose explanations

However, AI Never Owns Outcomes. Humans Do.

You will review, validate, and be accountable for what is ultimately communicated.

CI/CD & Continuous Delivery Participation

This role participates in CI/CD practices to ensure analytics outputs are safe and correct.

You Will
• Contribute to CI pipelines for analytics logic, testing, and validation
• Validate reports before release
• Participate in release readiness by ensuring that reporting quality gates are met

You will not own deployment mechanics, but you are expected to work effectively within a CI/CD environment.

Human-in-the-Loop Responsibility

We design systems where judgment is not optional.

You Will Be Responsible For
• Reviewing AI-generated insights before they are published or shared
• Ensuring accuracy of metrics and narratives
• Communicating uncertainty clearly when inputs or outcomes are ambiguous
• Owning the downstream impact of reporting and interpretation

Requirements

We’re Looking For Someone Who Has
• Experience working with analytics, reporting, dashboards, or data pipelines
• Strong SQL skills and the ability to write clear, maintainable queries
• Understanding of metrics design, validation, and data quality principles
• Ability to explain outcomes clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
• Strong attention to detail and a high bar for correctness
• Comfort working in environments where AI is used as an assistive tool (with human accountability)
• Familiarity with CI practices such as testing, validation checks, and review workflows
• A mindset focused on trust, transparency, and continuous improvement

Nice to Have

Bonus Points If You Have Experience With
• Python or another language used for analytics automation
• BI tools (e.g., Looker, Power BI, Tableau, Mode, Metabase)
• Data transformation frameworks (e.g., dbt)
• Monitoring and anomaly detection techniques
• Working in operational domains such as Fraud, Risk, Compliance, Security, or Asset Intelligence
• Writing documentation and metric definitions for long-term reuse

What Success Looks Like

Success In This Role Means
• Reports and dashboards remain correct, stable, and trusted
• Stakeholders receive clear narratives, not just raw metrics
• Anomalies are detected early, investigated quickly, and explained confidently
• Analytics outputs are delivered safely through validation gates
• Data becomes a tool for decision-making, not confusion

Why This Role Matters

We believe analytics is not just about building dashboards - it’s about delivering truth, clarity, and confidence in high-impact environments.

If you care about correctness, communication, and building intelligence that drives real outcomes, we’d love to hear from you.

jobs.title: Senior Data Engineer (Graph DB specialist)
jobs.description:
About Mindcurv (Part of Accenture Song)
We help our customers rethink their digital business, experiences, and technology to navigate the new digital reality. We do this by designing sustainable and accountable solutions for humans living in a digital world. Mindcurv holistically covers the market’s need to digitalise business processes and customer experiences and take advantage of the cloud following DevOps and agile principles.

Your role

We are seeking a highly skilled Data Engineer with expertise in graph databases to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in data engineering, graph querying languages, and data modeling, with a keen interest in leveraging cutting-edge technologies like vector databases and LLMs to drive functional objectives. Your responsibilities will include:
• Design, implement, and maintain ETL pipelines to prepare data for graph-based structures.
• Develop and optimize graph database solutions using querying languages such as Cypher, SPARQL, or GQL. Neo4J DB experience is preferred.
• Build and maintain ontologies and knowledge graphs, ensuring efficient and scalable data modeling.
• Integrate vector databases and implement similarity search techniques, with a focus on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) methodologies and GraphRAG.
• Collaborate with data scientists and engineers to operationalize machine learning models and integrate with graph databases.
• Work with Large Language Models (LLMs) to achieve functional and business objectives.
• Ensure data quality, integrity, and security while delivering robust and scalable solutions.
• Communicate effectively with stakeholders to understand business requirements and deliver solutions that meet objectives.

Who you are
• Experience: At least 5 years of hands-on experience in data engineering. With 2 years of experience working with Graph DB.
• Programming: Proficiency in Python and PySpark programming.
• Querying: Advanced knowledge of Cypher, SPARQL, or GQL querying languages.
• ETL Processes: Expertise in designing and optimizing ETL processes for graph structures.
• Data Modeling: Strong skills in creating ontologies and knowledge graphs.Presenting data for Graph RAG based solutions
• Vector Databases: Understanding of similarity search techniques and RAG implementations.
• LLMs: Experience working with Large Language Models for functional objectives.
• Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Nice-to-Have Qualifications
• Cloud Platforms: Experience with Azure analytics platforms, including Function Apps, Logic Apps, and Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS).
• Graph Analytics: Familiarity with graph algorithms and analytics.
• Agile Methodology: Hands-on experience working in Agile teams and processes.
• Machine Learning: Understanding of machine learning models and their implementation.

What do we offer you?

Perks, like drinks and snacks, pizza sessions, gaming nights and sensational parties, a pension scheme and a great salary, we’ve got them all.

And if you crave an intellectual challenge, Mindcurv has you covered. Interesting projects involving the latest, hyper innovative tech. An agile, entrepreneurial environment, with lots of freedom and no politics. Work-life balance, a culture of transparency and a management team with their ears to the ground.

We typically are the trailblazers and innovators. A hybrid way of working in which you can work from home. You just come into one of our offices in Essen, Cologne, Düsseldorf, Munich, Frankfurt a.M., Hamburg, Jena, Utrecht, Madrid, Cochin, Coimbatore or Trivandrum when it adds value to be on-site. Looking for a glance behind the scenes?

Our high performers

You know who really thrive with us? Self-starters, team-players and continuous learners, with an uncanny ability to handle ambiguity. We’ll equip you with everything you need to succeed, help you explore the length and breadth of your domain and provide you with constant growth opportunities, to enrich your career.

Ready for change?

Are you ready for the next step in your career? For a role in which you can be fully yourself and bring out the best? In yourself, your colleagues and your clients? Don’t wait any longer and apply for this job right now.

jobs.title: Software Engineer II
jobs.description:
Our story

At Alight, we believe a company’s success starts with its people. At our core, we Champion People, help our colleagues Grow with Purpose and true to our name we encourage colleagues to “Be Alight.”



Our Values:

Champion People – be empathetic and help create a place where everyone belongs.

Grow with purpose – Be inspired by our higher calling of improving lives.

Be Alight – act with integrity, be real and empower others.



It’s why we’re so driven to connect passion with purpose. Alight helps clients gain a benefits advantage while building a healthy and financially secure workforce by unifying the benefits ecosystem across health, wealth, wellbeing, absence management and navigation.



With a comprehensive total rewards package, continuing education and training, and tremendous potential with a growing global organization, Alight is the perfect place to put your passion to work.



Join our team if you Champion People, want to Grow with Purpose through acting with integrity and if you embody the meaning of Be Alight.



Learn more at careers.alight.com.



Software Engineer with 4 years of experience in ETL and Data Warehousing. The ideal candidate will have strong hands-on expertise in SQL development across Hive, Spark, and Impala, along with a solid understanding of cloud ecosystems. This role requires proficiency in big data technologies and scripting, as well as flexibility to collaborate during U.S. business hours. AWS certification is a plus.



Core Responsibilities:

Design, develop, and maintain ETL workflows and data pipelines for large-scale data processing.Write and optimize medium-complexity SQL queries on Hive, Spark, and Impala for use case-driven scenarios.Develop and maintain scripts using HIVE, PySpark, Spark, and Scala for data transformation and processing.Implement and troubleshoot Unix Shell scripts for automation and system tasks.Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data integrity and performance.Work in alignment with U.S. business hours for meetings and deliverables.Contribute to cloud-based data solutions and ensure best practices in data engineering.

Requirements:

Experience: 4 years in ETL and Data Warehousing.



Technical Skills:

Strong SQL skills with Hive, Spark, and Impala.Proficiency in HIVE / PySpark / Spark / Scala scripting.Good understanding of Unix Shell scripting.Conceptual knowledge of cloud ecosystems (AWS preferred).

Certifications: AWS Certified applicants are highly desirable.

Soft Skills: Strong problem-solving ability, adaptability, and communication skills.

Work Hours: Flexibility to overlap with U.S. business hours.



Alight requires all virtual interviews to be conducted on video.



Flexible Working

So that you can be your best at work and home, we consider flexible working arrangements wherever possible.  Alight has been a leader in the flexible workspace and “Top 100 Company for Remote Jobs” 5 years in a row.





Benefits

We offer programs and plans for a healthy mind, body, wallet and life because it’s important our benefits care for the whole person. Options include a variety of health coverage options, wellbeing and support programs, retirement, vacation and sick leave, maternity, paternity & adoption leave, continuing education and training as well as several voluntary benefit options.



By applying for a position with Alight, you understand that, should you be made an offer, it will be contingent on your undergoing and successfully completing a background check consistent with Alight’s employment policies. Background checks may include some or all the following based on the nature of the position: SSN/SIN validation, education verification, employment verification, and criminal check, search against global sanctions and government watch lists, credit check, and/or drug test.  You will be notified during the hiring process which checks are required by the position.



Our commitment to Inclusion

We celebrate differences and believe in fostering an environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and supported. We know that diverse teams are stronger, more innovative, and more successful.



At Alight, we welcome and embrace all individuals, regardless of their background, and are dedicated to creating a culture that enables every employee to thrive. Join us in building a brighter, more inclusive future.



As part of this commitment, Alight will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations for the hiring process. If reasonable accommodation is needed, please contact alightcareers@alight.com.



Equal Opportunity Policy Statement

Alight is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and does not discriminate against anyone based on sex, race, color, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital or domestic partner status, citizenship, military or veteran status, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, genetic information, or any other legally protected characteristics or conduct covered by federal, state, or local law.   In addition, we take affirmative action to employ, disabled persons, disabled veterans and other covered veterans.



Alight provides reasonable accommodations to the known limitations of otherwise qualified employees and applicants for employment with disabilities and sincerely held religious beliefs, practices and observances, unless doing so would result in undue hardship. Applicants for employment may request a reasonable accommodation/modification by contacting their  recruiter.



Authorization to work in the Employing Country

Applicants for employment in the country in which they are applying (Employing Country) must have work authorization that does not now or in the future require sponsorship of a visa for employment authorization in the Employing Country and with Alight.



Note, this job description does not restrict management's right to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities of this job to other entities; including but not limited to subsidiaries, partners, or purchasers of Alight business units.





We offer you a competitive total rewards package, continuing education & training, and tremendous potential with a growing worldwide organization.
 


DISCLAIMER:


Nothing in this job description restricts management's right to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities of this job to other entities; including but not limited to subsidiaries, partners, or purchasers of Alight business units.

jobs.title: Executive Support Specialist – Digital Enablement
jobs.description:
This a Full Remote job, the offer is available from: India, Malaysia

Our people are foundational to our strategy– they are not ‘resources’ that we leverage! Our People team (formerly Human Resources) partners with leaders to engage and attract high performing, diverse, entrepreneurial talent. We continuously challenge ourselves to be BOLD in our thinking, design and delivery of people solutions that enable our people to be the best that they can be and drive exceptional ResMed success.

Let’s talk about the team:

We are seeking a tech-enabled Executive Support Specialist to transform how administrative support is delivered across our organization. Based in Kuala Lumpur, this role is part of our larger Shared Services team, working closely with colleagues across regions and reporting directly to a manager in San Diego, CA, USA.

The ideal candidate may come from one of two paths:

• An Executive Assistant/Administrative professional excited to adopt automation and AI to modernize support.
• An Automation/Workflow specialist eager to apply technical expertise in an executive-facing environment.

As an Executive Support Specialist – Digital Enablement, you will:

• Provide centralized executive assistant–style support.
• Apply your expertise in Power Automate, AI tools, and workflow optimization to redesign how administrative tasks are managed (from scheduling and travel booking to purchase order approvals and transactional workflows).
• Create digital-first solutions that reduce friction, increase efficiency, and elevate the employee and manager experience.
• Contribute to engagement activities and build Shared Services capability by coaching teammates in automation.

Let’s talk about the role:

Automation & AI Enablement
• Design, test, and maintain automated workflows for administrative and operational processes, including:
• Calendar management and meeting coordination.
• Travel booking and approvals.
• Purchase order (PO) and expense approval processes.
• Document routing, signatures, and records management.
• Broader operational and administrative workflows.
• Implement AI-powered solutions (Copilot, GPT, bots) to improve efficiency for executive assistants, managers, and employees.
• Continuously assess workflows to identify opportunities for automation, simplification, and scalability.

Executive Administrative Support (Technology-Enabled)
• Provide centralized EA-style support for executives remotely, balancing hands-on administrative tasks with automation-enabled workflows.
• Anticipate executive needs and deliver proactive, seamless support for calendar, travel, approvals, and expenses.
• Partner closely with other Executive Assistants and leaders to design workflows that are both efficient and intuitive.

Employee & Manager Experience
• Ensure automation solutions make work simpler and more intuitive for executives, assistants, and employees.
• Champion human-centered solutions that enhance, not diminish, the personal experience of support.
• Act as a trusted partner within the Administrative Assistant People Experience organization.

Engagement & Events
• Support employee engagement activities (wellness, recognition, culture events).
• Bring presence, passion, and commitment when supporting live, in-person events.

Collaboration, Training & Schedule
• Work as part of a global Shared Services team, collaborating across regions.
• Report directly to a manager in San Diego, USA, ensuring global alignment.
• Train and coach colleagues by sharing automation and AI expertise, enabling teammates to design and deliver their own solutions.
• Work onsite in the Kuala Lumpur office two days per week (Wednesdays & Thursdays) and work from home the remainder of the week.
• Adjust working hours on Tuesdays and Fridays (early morning to mid-afternoon) to overlap with San Diego colleagues.

Let’s talk about you:

Required (either track acceptable):

Track A (EA-first):
• 2–4 years of experience as an Executive Assistant, Administrative Assistant, or Shared Services or professional, supporting executives or senior leaders.
• Strong understanding of executive workflows (calendar, travel, expenses, approvals).
• Demonstrated curiosity and willingness to learn automation and AI tools.

Track B (Tech-first):
• 1–3 years of experience in automation, workflow design, or administrative process optimization.
• Strong expertise in Microsoft Power Automate (flows, connectors, Outlook, Teams, SharePoint integrations).
• Strong expertise with AI productivity tools (Copilot, GPT, chatbots).
• Willingness to develop EA support skills (confidentiality, executive presence, proactive admin support).

Shared Competencies (all candidates):
• Excellent collaboration skills; ability to partner across geographies.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills; confident and discreet when engaging with executives.
• Strong communicator who takes ownership and initiative, is a relentless improver with customer empathy, and is results-oriented.
• Passionate about employee and manager experience, eager to design solutions that make work easier.
• Problem-solving mindset with the ability to translate pain points into elegant, scalable solutions.

Preferred:
• Experience supporting or partnering with executive assistants or administrative professionals.
• Knowledge of HRIS, finance, or travel systems (Workday, Concur, Egencia, etc.).
• Exposure to employee experience or culture initiatives.
• Degree in Human Resources or related field (considered an asset).

Why Join Us?

This is not a traditional EA or automation role — it is a hybrid, future-facing position. You will:
• Be at the forefront of digital transformation in administrative support.
• Develop skills across executive support, workflow automation, and global collaboration.
• Shape the future of how executives are supported — blending the personal touch of an EA with the efficiency of AI and automation.

Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! We commit to respond to every applicant.

This offer from "MEDIFOX DAN" has been enriched by Jobgether.com and got a 75% flex score.

jobs.title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
jobs.description:
Achieving our goals starts with supporting yours. Grow your career, access top-tier health and wellness benefits, build lasting connections with your team and our customers, and travel the world using our extensive route network.

Come join us to create what’s next. Let’s define tomorrow, together.

Description

United's Digital Technology team is comprised of many talented individuals all working together with cutting-edge technology to build the best airline in the history of aviation. Our team designs, develops and maintains massively scaling technology solutions brought to life with innovative architectures, data analytics, and digital solutions.

Job overview and responsibilities

The Cloud Infrastructure Engineer is responsible for implementation of large scale critical cloud infrastructure projects. They will work with the Principal Cloud Architects to implement core projects on United Private Cloud (AWS, GCP, Azure) and cloud related SaaS and PaaS deliveries. They will also work with our AppDev and DevOps partners to facilitate application migrations to the cloud. The engineer role will need to work with cross functional teams to enable and provide a mature and scalable experience for delivering workloads to the cloud infrastructure. The engineer will follow security and governance best practices and contribute to the Enterprise Architecture standards for cloud.

• Contribute to the design, Implementation and support of cloud infrastructure projects.
• Participate in POC’s on new tools based on based requirements.
• Collaborate with team members and foster a collaborative and innovative environment.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams (DevOps, Security, DBA, Architects) to ensure timely completion of projects.
• Automate cloud infrastructure deployments and configurations using infrastructure as code (IaC) tools like Terraform.
• Provide technical support and consultation for the United Cloud environment.
• Participate in incident response and incident management in United Cloud environment.

This position is offered on local terms and conditions. Expatriate assignments and sponsorship for employment visas, even on a time-limited visa status, will not be awarded. This position is for United Airlines Business Services Pvt. Ltd - a wholly owned subsidiary of United Airlines Inc.

Qualifications
What’s needed to succeed (Minimum Qualifications):

• Bachelor's degree or 4 years of relevant work experience
• 5+ years of experience in Cloud or equivalent Data Center engineering
• AWS Foundational Certification AWS Associate Certification
• Extensive hands-on experience in the design, implementation, and support of cloud infrastructure.
• Proven track record of working alongside architects and cross-functional teams to develop and implement large-scale critical infrastructure projects, ensuring their successful completion.
• In-depth knowledge of widely used AWS services, including but not limited to EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, and VPC.
• Skilled in designing and automating activities across multiple AWS services, with a focus on end-to-end implementation.
• Proficiency in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as AWS CloudFormation and Terraform.
• Expertise in using Boto3 Python API to manage resources across the cloud environment and develop reusable modules based on specific use cases.
• Excellent communication and collaboration abilities, capable of working effectively with architects, developers, and other cross-functional teams.
• Problem-solving mindset with a proactive approach to identifying and addressing technical challenges
• Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
• Containers & Workloads: Knowledge of securing Docker, Kubernetes (EKS), and serverless (Lambda) environments.
• Tooling Ecosystem: Hands-on with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Harness), artifact management (Artifactory), and monitoring (Dynatrace, CloudWatch).
• Incident Response: Experience in troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, and post-mortem processes in cloud environments.

What will help you propel from the pack (Preferred Qualifications):

• 8+ Years experience
• AWS Professional Certification
• Ability to stay current with the latest cloud technologies and best practices, continuously seeking to improve cloud infrastructure and processes.
• Experience with monitoring, logging, and alerting solutions for cloud environments. Familiarity with security best practices and compliance requirements in cloud computing.
• Ability to balance leadership responsibilities with hands-on technical work, ensuring high-quality delivery of cloud solutions.
• Hands-on experience with incident management, troubleshooting, and post-mortem processes for production environments.

 I am looking for an experienced cybersecurity technical writer or GRC consultant to help create polished, professional client-facing materials that I can use in consulting proposals and business development.

Framework expertise

Professional positioning and structure

Clean, professional formatting

ISO 27001 implementation projects

SOC 2 readiness projects

 Cybersecurity consulting GRC (Governance, Risk, Compliance) specifically ISO27001 and SOC2 Security technical writing

 My professional background and experience My service offerings and positioning Guidance on structure and messaging Feedback and directionThis is a collaboration — not content generation from scratch.

 Professional Realistic Consulting-grade quality Clean formatting Client-facing ready
Preferred Qualifications:

Strong preference for candidates who have worked as:

 ISO 27001 consultant SOC 2 consultant GRC consultant Security auditor Security compliance specialistContract duration of 1 to 3 months. with 40 hours per week.

Job Title: GL Accounting Billing/Collections

Level: Senior Associate

Position: 1

Role Summary

GL Accounting Billing/Collections role will support billing processes, including invoice generation and manual billing adjustments, manage collection activities such as accounts receivable aging tracking and escalation, handle cash payment applications, and assist with other month-end close tasks like reconciling the billing engine with the ledger.

Key Responsibilities
• Perform manual billing calculations to handle adjustments, credits, refunds, and exception cases not automated by billing systems.
• Generate accurate and timely invoices, ensuring compliance with pricing agreements, contracts, and billing cycles.
• Manage and prioritize billing query queues received via Slack, JIRA, and AR mailbox, providing clear communication and timely resolutions.
• Collaborate with internal teams and customers to resolve billing disputes, discrepancies, and invoice-related escalations effectively.
• Monitor accounts receivable aging, drive collections activities including follow-ups and escalation, and reduce outstanding balances.
• Process cash applications by accurately matching payments to open invoices and resolving payment discrepancies.
• Conduct month-end billing reconciliation activities, including matching billing engine data to the general ledger to ensure accuracy.
• Prepare and maintain reports on billing operations, revenue recognition, collections status, and aging analysis to inform management decisions.
• Assist in identifying process improvement opportunities to automate billing, collections, and cash application workflows.
• Support compliance with accounting policies, internal controls, and audit requirements related to revenue and receivables.
• Provide support for system upgrades, testing new billing features, and collaborating with IT or Finance Analytics teams as needed.
• Communicate proactively with cross-functional teams such as Finance, Customer Success, and Legal to align billing and collections strategies.
• Maintain up-to-date documentation on billing procedures, dispute management, and cash application workflows.

Required Qualifications
• Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
• 3-5 years of professional experience in billing, accounts receivable, revenue operations, or accounting.
• Proficiency with Excel and Google Sheets, including advanced functions, pivot tables, and data analysis.
• Experience writing and executing SQL queries or similar data extraction/reporting tools to analyze billing and payment data.
• Strong knowledge of billing processes, invoice generation, cash application, collections, and dispute resolution.
• Familiarity with managing billing queries through communication platforms such as Slack, JIRA, or email.
• Ability to reconcile billing data with general ledger entries accurately.
• Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with experience working cross-functionally.

Preferred Qualifications
• Experience with billing or financial systems such as Oracle, SAP, NetSuite, Zuora, or similar ERP/billing platforms.
• Knowledge of accounts receivable aging, collections strategies, and dispute resolution best practices.
• Familiarity with data visualization or analytics tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker) to support revenue and billing analytics.
• Experience with automation or workflow tools that improve billing, collections, or cash application efficiency.
• Understanding of key accounting principles related to revenue recognition and month-end close processes.
• Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
• Proactive problem-solving skills and a continuous improvement mindset.

Tools
• Microsoft Excel (advanced features, pivot tables)
• Google Sheets
• SQL databases and querying tools (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, or built-in SQL editors in BI tools)
• Billing and ERP systems (e.g., Oracle, SAP, NetSuite, Zuora)
• Workflow and issue tracking platforms (Slack, JIRA, email systems)
• Data visualization and analytics tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker)
• Financial reconciliation tools or modules within ERP systems
• Automation tools or scripting languages for workflow optimization (e.g., Python, VBA)
• Communication and documentation tools (Confluence, Google Docs, Microsoft Teams

jobs.title: Digital Finance & AI Automation
jobs.description:
About Prosperion:

Prosperion Consulting is a results-oriented firm dedicated to empowering businesses to achieve sustainable growth and operational excellence. We bring insightful perspectives and tailored solutions to address the unique challenges and opportunities within the Business Consultancy, Financial Services, and Management Consultancy landscapes. Our approach is built on deep industry expertise, a commitment to collaboration, and a passion for delivering measurable impact for our clients. We pride ourselves on fostering strong, long-term partnerships, guiding organizations through complexity, and helping them realize their full potential. Just as Prosperion Consulting emphasizes a focus on tangible results and client success, so too do we prioritize delivering practical, effective strategies that drive real value.

Job Summary:

We are looking for a tech-forward Finance Professional who is obsessed with efficiency. Your goal is to take Prosperion’s accounting and financial operations into the AI era. You won't be building AI from scratch; instead, you will be leveraging "ready-to-use" AI platforms (like Anthropic’s Claude) to automate repetitive tasks, analyze data, and ensure 100% compliance for our clients.

Responsibilities:

AI Integration: Use Claude and other AI tools to automate the drafting of financial reports, tax summaries, and audit notes.

Workflow Optimization: Identify "bottlenecks" in our current accounting processes (GST filing, data entry, reconciliations) and use AI tools to solve them.

Prompt Engineering: Master the art of "talking to AI" to get high-quality, accurate financial outputs that require minimal human correction.

Data Cleanup & Analysis: Use AI to quickly categorize expenses, spot anomalies in bank statements, and summarize complex financial regulations (MCA/GST updates).

Tool Management: Stay updated on the latest AI tools for finance (e.g., AI-integrated Excel add-ins, automated document parsers) and implement them for our clients.

Required Skills and Qualifications:

Finance Foundation: A degree in Commerce (B.Com/M.Com), an Inter-CA, or an MBA in Finance. You must understand debits, credits, and Indian compliance (GST/TDS) deeply.

AI Fluency: You should already be using tools like Claude, ChatGPT, or Perplexity in your daily work. You know how to get these tools to perform complex reasoning.

Excel Mastery: Advanced Excel skills are a must (VLOOKUPs are basic; you should know Power Query or how to use AI to write complex formulas).

Curiosity: A natural "hacker" mindset. If a task takes 3 hours, you are the person who finds a way to do it in 15 minutes using a tool.

Preferred Background

Experience in an accounting firm, KPO, or a finance-ops role where you have already tried to "automate yourself" out of boring tasks.

Familiarity with Indian accounting software like Tally Prime, Zoho Books, or SAP.

More about careers at Prosperion Consulting on https://prosperionconsulting.com/careers/

jobs.title: Finance Head - India
jobs.via: HR Software For Growing Businesses | Freshteam
jobs.description:
About Syfe


Syfe is APAC's largest and fastest-growing digital wealth platform, trusted with over US$10 billion in assets. We are fundamentally changing how hundreds of thousands of people across Asia-Pacific build wealth through a holistic approach to managing money rather than just pushing investment products. Backed by world-class investors and recognised as a leader in wealthtech, we are a team of passionate builders creating the future of wealth management.

Location: Gurgaon / Bengaluru | Hybrid Work (3 Days From Office).

Role Overview
The Financial Controller will serve as the Finance Head for India while also playing a key role in the financial consolidation, reporting, and accounting for Syfe’s international entities in Singapore, Hong Kong, and Australia. This role requires deep expertise in IFRS, end-to-end controllership, and experience working in a Fintech or highly regulated digital financial services environment.



What You’ll Do

India Finance Leadership

Act as the Finance Head for India, overseeing all financial operations, statutory reporting, and compliance.

Manage India entity financial statements, audits, tax filings, and regulatory submissions.

Ensure strong internal controls, risk management, and financial governance.

Global Financial Consolidation & Reporting

Lead monthly and quarterly accounting and bookkeeping across India, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Australia.

Deliver accurate and timely management reporting, board packs, and financial analyses.

Maintain alignment with group accounting policies and IFRS standards.

Liaise with auditors for group and entity-level audits.

Multi-Entity Accounting & Process Excellence

Oversee day-to-day accounting across regions, ensuring compliance with IFRS and local statutory requirements.

Strengthen global accounting processes, financial controls, and reporting automation.

Collaborate cross-functionally to support new product launches and assess financial impacts.

Drive improvements in reconciliations, the chart of accounts, and month-end close timelines.

Stakeholder Management

Work closely with the HOD Finance and senior leadership across geographies.

Serve as the primary finance partner for India and cross-regional teams.

Coordinate with auditors, tax advisors, regulatory authorities, and banking partners.



What We’re Looking For


Must-Have:

Chartered Accountant (CA) with minimum 10 years of post-qualification experience.Mandatory Fintech industry experience.Strong technical expertise in IFRS, group consolidation, and multi-entity accounting.Experience working in a global environment.Proven track record in statutory audits, financial reporting, and controllership.
Preferred:Experience in high-growth or scaling environments.

Exposure to MAS, ASIC, or other APAC regulatory frameworks.

Knowledge of ERP systems and automation tools.




Skills & Competencies

Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

Excellent communication and cross-cultural collaboration.

Hands-on, detail-oriented, and skilled at managing complexity.

Ability to operate independently and influence key decisions.

High integrity and commitment to sound financial governance.



Recruiting Process (estimated duration: 4 to 6 weeks)

If your application is shortlisted, please expect:

Initial screening and fitment (15 min) – Recruiter Phone Call.

Interview 1 ( 60 Min ) - Intro Round With Hiring Manager.

Interview 2 (60 min) – Joint Interview With Internal Panel.

Interview 3 (60 min) – Case study / hands-on problem-solving round.

Interview 4 (60 min) – Leadership and stakeholder alignment discussion.

Interview 5 (60 min) - Final Round With COO.

HR Discussion & Culture Fit.

Background and Reference Checks.



Due to the volume of applications, we regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Credit Financing & Securitization - Officer
jobs.description:
Whether you’re at the start of your career or looking to discover your next adventure, your story begins here. At Citi, you’ll have the opportunity to expand your skills and make a difference at one of the world’s most global banks. We’re fully committed to supporting your growth and development from the start with extensive on-the-job training and exposure to senior leaders, as well as more traditional learning. You’ll also have the chance to give back and make a positive impact where we live and work through volunteerism.




The Credit Risk Analyst position will hold a role within the Real Estate & Structured Credit Underwriting (RESCU) team administering portfolio of asset securitization transactions across the ABS class (including auto, credit card, unsecured consumer loans, etc). We are looking for solid individual contributor with good problem solving and analytical thinking in the ABS space. This role will provide unique opportunities for mastering in-depth collateral and deal analysis.




In this role, you’re expected to:



Accountability for ensuring strong analysis of the assigned portfolio ensuring consistencies with industry leading practices and compliance with all internal risk policies, standards and procedures.



Assist analysts and associates to perform periodic monitoring of the portfolio.



Ensure maintenance of organized credit files for credit, regulatory and compliance purposes and ensuring compliance with WCR policies and processes.



Analyze the credit risk by performing tasks that includes reviewing deal documents & legal documentation.



Prepare credit approval memos analyzing the risk associated with the collateral quality.



Analyze periodic data & report for any discrepancy in the data. 



Collect, organize and analyze data from internal and external sources.



Enhance efficiency through automation & technical skills. 



Maintain regular and open communication with internal as well as external team members on various tasks. 



Understand the domain and implement quick fix solutions to align the development activities with dynamic business needs. 



Stay abreast with the latest developments in the structured finance market and understand on their potential impact.




As a successful candidate, you’d ideally have the following skills and exposure:



Bachelor’s/University degree or equivalent experience.



1-3 years of credit, portfolio management or product experience (preferably in structured finance/securitization) in a financial institution, rating agency, financial guarantor or asset management firm.



Requires good analytical skills in order to filter, prioritize and validate potentially complex material from multiple sources.



Knowledge of wholesale loan (syndicated / bilateral) product and business, including an understanding of legal loan documentation, loan / funding mechanics, deal structure, funds flows, etc.



Excellent organizational skills, prioritizing capabilities, attention to detail, and the ability to complete assignments within required deadlines in a fast-paced environment.



Pro-active disposition with ability to work autonomously and within a team.



Creative thinker who is adept at taking an analytical approach to problem solving.



Strong attention to detail – the candidate will have responsibility for reviewing credit agreements / legal documents to ensure that all risk and business points are covered.



Excellent oral and written communications skills enabling the candidate to effectively interact with internal and external constituents of all levels.



Strong Microsoft Office skills. Modelling experience is a plus.




Education:



Bachelor’s/University degree or equivalent experience

jobs.title: Junior Finance Associate
jobs.description:
Junior - Finance Associate

Have you ever wondered how the physical goods we consume daily (dairy, snacks, or your favourite choco chip ice cream) reaches you in time? Or how elegant furniture, electronic equipment, and gadgets you use are delivered in the best possible condition at your doorstep. 


Their journey spans from getting sourced, manufactured, and stored to getting moved in a highly intricate supply chain network across the globe before it gets delivered. Global brands face the major challenge of not having visibility of their raw materials, which delays production and, in turn, delays customer shipments or tracking shipments across various modalities and multiple geographies. 


Intugine Technologies is one of the most trusted solution partners catering to global brands. Our best-in-class visibility platform helps brands gain comprehensive visibility over their supply chain across modalities(air/land/sea/rail). Our solutions have helped eliminate operational inefficiencies, reduce logistics costs, improve OTIF, order to delivery TAT, minimise dwell time and detention costs.


Intugine is a proud partner of the National Logistics Policy, an initiative by the Government of India. With this, we provide brands with additional information via their integrations with FASTag, Port Community System, and Freight Operations Information System. 


Today Intugine works with 75+ global enterprises like GE Healthcare, Signify, Flipkart, Mahindra Logistics, Titan, Diageo, Ultratech Cement, Bridgestone, and GMMCO.

Own day-to-day accounting operations including bookkeeping and ledger maintenance.Handle accounts payable & receivable, vendor payments, and customer collections.Track and follow up on outstanding invoices and resolve billing discrepancies.Manage bank reconciliations, expense tracking, Invoicing, etcSupport GST, TDS, and other statutory compliances with timely filings.Assist in monthly closures, MIS preparation, and financial reporting.Coordinate with auditors for compliance and audits.Maintain proper documentation for invoices, credit notes, PO and Agreements.Support ad-hoc finance and operations tasks as required.

Master’s degree in Commerce, Accounting, or Finance1–2 years of hands-on experience in accounting or finance (Who has worked in a CA firm or Start Up )Working knowledge of GST, TDS, invoicing, and reconciliationsProficiency in Excel / Google Sheets (formulas, pivots, basic analysis)Experience with accounting tools like Zoho Books, QuickBooks, or similarAbility to work independently, take ownership, and adapt quicklyStrong attention to detail and problem-solving mindset

jobs.title: Group Financial Controls Manager- IT Controls
jobs.description:
Why BT Group?

BT Group was the world’s first telco and our heritage in the sector is unrivalled. As home to several of the UK’s most recognised and cherished brands – BT, EE, Openreach and Plusnet, we have always played a critical role in creating the future, and we have reached an inflection point in the transformation of our business.

Over the next two years, we will complete the UK’s largest and most successful digital infrastructure project – connecting more than 25 million premises to full fibre broadband. Together with our heavy investment in 5G, we play a central role in revolutionising how people connect with each other.

While we are through the most capital-intensive phase of our fibre investment, meaning we can reward our shareholders for their commitment and patience, we are absolutely focused on how we organise ourselves in the best way to serve our customers in the years to come. This includes radical simplification of systems, structures, and processes on a huge scale. Together with our application of AI and technology, we are on a path to creating the UK’s best telco, reimagining the customer experience and relationship with one of this country’s biggest infrastructure companies.

Change on the scale we will all experience in the coming years is unprecedented. BT Group is committed to being the driving force behind improving connectivity for millions and there has never been a more exciting time to join a company and leadership team with the skills, experience, creativity, and passion to take this company into a new era.

Why this job matters

The transformation of BT’s financial control framework is a key objective of the BT board and Executive Committee.

This role is critical in ensuring that transformation programs deliver improvements in the efficiency and effectiveness our financial controls and maintain the integrity of BT’s financial reporting.

What You’ll Be Doing – Your Accountabilities

Working together with both operational and transformation teams from across the company you will set, objectives, define accountabilities and drive towards best practice for finance control delivery within major transformation programs.

Actively horizon scanning to identify transformation programs that may impact BT’s financial control framework and IT systems and ensuring appropriate risk / impact assessments are completedPro-actively identifying relevant stakeholders and raising their awareness of financial control through training, policies and guidanceWorking with transformation teams, unit compliance teams and other key stakeholders to ensure clear accountability for financial control within transformation programsProviding transformation teams with expert guidance on best practice for financial control design and implementation and ensuring compliance with relevant internal and external policies and regulationReporting on the status of financial control delivery within major transformation programs

The Skills You’ll Need To Succeed

Good knowledge of SOX and ITGC frameworks including design and test of operating effectivenessMatrix working: Comfortable working in a large organisation where activities are managed according to a matrix structure. Able to work collaboratively and influence others to achieve goalsCommunication: translating technical and business challenges to senior leaders and key stakeholders through both oral and written communicationTechnical Mindset: ability to understand, dedupe and explain complex process, systems and controlsAbility to Challenge: bias towards bold and confident thinking, challenging the status quo where appropriatePragmatism: able to deal with ambiguity, identify resources to implement and determine pragmatic solutionsPrioritisation: able to prioritise work and work independently on core deliverables

Leadership Accountabilities

Leading inclusively and safely: Interacting respectfully and effectively across diverse teamsOwning outcomes: Collaborating effectively across teams, functions and business units to deliver effective financial controls for BTGrowth mindset: Being open to explore and learn new technology and ways of working and taking responsibility for own developmentBuilding for the Future: Proactively monitoring and driving adoption of developments in technology and best practice from internal and external sources

Experience You’d Be Expected To Have

Mandatory - Professionally qualified accountant, risk manager, auditor or equivalent experiencePreferred – Experience of Telco SectorPreferred – Experience working in large complex organisations

Key decisions

Recommends policies and standards for financial controlRecommends whether transformation programmes have met the required standards for financial control

About Us

BT Group was the world’s first telco and our heritage in the sector is unrivalled. As home to several of the UK’s most recognised and cherished brands – BT, EE, Openreach and Plusnet, we have always played a critical role in creating the future, and we have reached an inflection point in the transformation of our business.

Over the next two years, we will complete the UK’s largest and most successful digital infrastructure project – connecting more than 25 million premises to full fibre broadband. Together with our heavy investment in 5G, we play a central role in revolutionising how people connect with each other.

While we are through the most capital-intensive phase of our fibre investment, meaning we can reward our shareholders for their commitment and patience, we are absolutely focused on how we organise ourselves in the best way to serve our customers in the years to come. This includes radical simplification of systems, structures, and processes on a huge scale. Together with our application of AI and technology, we are on a path to creating the UK’s best telco, reimagining the customer experience and relationship with one of this country’s biggest infrastructure companies.

Change on the scale we will all experience in the coming years is unprecedented. BT Group is committed to being the driving force behind improving connectivity for millions and there has never been a more exciting time to join a company and leadership team with the skills, experience, creativity, and passion to take this company into a new era.

A FEW POINTS TO NOTE:

Although these roles are listed as full-time, if you’re a job share partnership, work reduced hours, or any other way of working flexibly, please still get in touch.

We will also offer reasonable adjustments for the selection process if required, so please do not hesitate to inform us.

DON'T MEET EVERY SINGLE REQUIREMENT?

Studies have shown that women and people who are disabled, LGBTQ+, neurodiverse or from ethnic minority backgrounds are less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet every single qualification and criteria. We're committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace where everyone can be their best, so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every requirement on the Job Description, please apply anyway - you may just be the right candidate for this or other roles in our wider team.

Role:    

 We are seeking an experienced Manager who is passionate about financial modelling and driven by growth.   

You will work with a team of modelling professionals to create, analyse and optimise complex financial models, ensuring they are robust and reliable.  

Requirements    

Operations:    

 Managing a portfolio of active assignments   Lead in the creation and optimization of comprehensive financial models to support strategic decision-making.   Develop and refine assumptions, parameters, and scenarios under the guidance of Managers to ensure model robustness and reliability.   Review and interpret commerdcial documentation and ensure it is reflected accurately in the financial model.   Perform sensitivity analysis and stress testing to validate model assumptions and outputs, aiding clients in making informed decisions.   Contribute to the development and enhancement of our financial modeling methodologies and tools, driving continuous improvement and innovation.   Prepare comprehensive model documentation detailing assumptions, methodologies, and results.   Maintain meticulous records of modeling processes and methodologies to uphold our high standards and ensure consistency.   Lead in developing project plans, timelines, and resource allocation to ensure efficient and effective project delivery.     People:    

 Lead a team of modelling professionals   Train, motivate and manage junior team members on assignments   Collaborate closely with team members, learning and contributing to assignments.   Communicate model findings and insights effectively to clients and internal teams.   Simplify complex financial model issues to make them understandable for non-technical stakeholders.    Growth:    

 Supporting in the business development and growth ambitions of a key sector of the business   Lead in the development of client pitches   Lead client calls and build client relationships     Key Skills and Qualifications    

 We hire exceptional individuals who can demonstrate our core values of Drive, Innovation, Collaboration and Excellence.   (4-6) years of relevent experience in a financial role within a recognized advisory practice or large corporate setting.   A good appreciation of accounting concepts, an accounting qualification beneficial   An in-depth understanding of Project Finance concepts   Strong analytical skills with proven experience in building and operating financial models using Excel.   Degree qualified in a numerate discipline, with a postgraduate qualification in accounting or finance beneficial.   Experience with the FAST financial modeling standard is desirable.   Excellent written and verbal communication skills.   Ability to work independently and as part of a remote team    Benefits    

A competitive base salary with flexible working arrangements, including a mixture of office-based, working from home, and working on client sites.  

Significant opportunities for professional growth and development as we expand.  

Access to cutting-edge financial modelling tools and resources.  

Collaborative and supportive team culture

That’s why Finance is vital to the way we run our business. Gender neutral parental leaves 100% reimbursement under childcare assistance benefit (gender neutral) Sponsorship for Industry relevant certifications and education Employee Assistance Program for you and your family members Comprehensive Hospitalization Insurance for you and your dependents Accident and Term life Insurance Complementary Health screening for 35 yrs. and above Your key responsibilities Responsible for production and timely delivery of liquidity reports (i.e. LCR, NSFR,Stress testing, FR2052) Analyse variances and provide commentary. Good understanding of Balance sheet and regulatory reporting process. Ensure positive and productive engagement with stakeholders. Run ad-hoc analyses and communicate results to key stakeholders. Your skills and experience Strong data analysis skills & attention to detail Strong communication skills, both oral and written Fair understanding of various banking products Hands on experience of any of the reporting and analytical tools like Axiom, Tableau, SQL, Python, Alteryx Previous experience in production and review of BAU reports, validation and control, analysis and provision of business commentary (Preferred) Investment bank background (Preferred) Understanding of regulatory reporting within a Banking environment or Treasury function. Liquidity reporting experience preferable but not mandatory Education/ Qualifications Bachelor degree or equivalent qualification. How we’ll support you Training and development to help you excel in your career. Coaching and support from experts in your team. A culture of continuous learning to aid progression. For over 150 years, our dedication to being the Global Hausbank for our clients has been driven by our people – in around 60 countries and across more than 150 nationalities. Their deep understanding, insights, expertise, and passion help our clients navigate an increasingly complex world – be it in our Corporate Bank, our Private Bank, our Investment Bank or our Asset Management (DWS) division. Together we can make a great impact for our clients at home and abroad, securing their lasting success and financial security. More information at: Deutsche Bank Careers (db.com)

jobs.title: US LLC Admin, Compliance Ops, Banking, and Shopify Payments Support
jobs.description:
We are an established multi-country sports company based in Asia, with operations across multiple markets. We are now expanding into the US. Our Colorado LLC is already formed. We do not have a US office yet.

We need a reliable, detail-oriented admin and compliance operations partner to manage ongoing US LLC errands and help us get fully operational with banking and Shopify payouts.
This is not a one-time task. We want long-term support on a monthly retainer.

GOAL

Keep our Colorado LLC compliant and organized, and ensure our US banking and Shopify payouts run smoothly so we can launch and scale in the US.

COMPLIANCE SCOPE
You will help us stay in good standing and keep records organized. You are not acting as our attorney or CPA.

If a task requires legal or tax advice, you will flag it clearly and coordinate with our CPA or legal partner.

SCOPE OF WORK
Phase 1 (Week 1 to 2): Setup and stabilization

1) LLC admin review and document pack
• Review what we already have (Articles or Certificate, EIN if any, Operating Agreement if any, registered agent info)
• Create a clean, organized document pack for banking and platform verification
• Build a simple compliance and admin checklist (what to renew, when, where, and who owns it)
2) Compliance check and good standing setup
• Confirm Colorado Periodic Report status and upcoming deadlines
• Confirm registered agent status and renewal timing
• Confirm entity facts are consistent across documents (legal name, EIN, formation date, addresses)
Mention "Green Strawberry" twice in the 5th line of your proposal
• Confirm whether BOI reporting applies under current rules and document the conclusion
• Create a compliance calendar with links, logins, owners, and renewal dates
3) Address plan (no US office yet)

Recommend a compliant address setup for:
• State filings and registered agent
• IRS mail
• Banking requirements
• Shopify Payments and payout verification
• Customer returns address (coordinate with our operations or fulfillment owner)

Explain pros, cons, risks, and costs of options (registered agent address, virtual mailbox, warehouse or 3PL address, and other valid options).

Important:
We want a fully compliant setup. No fake addresses and no misrepresentation.

4) US business banking
• Help us choose the best bank for our scenario (Mercury preferred, but propose alternatives if better)
• Prepare the application package and guide onboarding steps
• Ensure access and admin setup for our team (users, roles, cards if available, security settings)
• Quick attention check: Mention "Green Strawberry" twice in the 5th line of your proposal.
• Track application status and any followups needed
5) Shopify Payments and payouts
• Guide Shopify Payments activation for our Colorado LLC
• Confirm business details, verification documents, and payout configuration
• Run a test order and test refund (with our Shopify owner) and confirm payouts work
• If Shopify Payments is blocked, propose best alternative gateway options and explain why
Phase 2 (Monthly retainer): Ongoing admin and support
• Track compliance and admin deadlines (Colorado Periodic Report, registered agent renewals, and any state requirements)
• Keep documentation organized and updated when needed
• Support banking admin requests (access changes, documents, troubleshooting)
• Support Shopify payouts troubleshooting and documentation updates
• Coordinate with our CPA and other partners when needed (you are not required to be a CPA or attorney)

DELIVERABLES
- "Current status" summary: what is already done, what is missing, and next steps
• Clean document pack for banking and platform verification
• Entity Facts Sheet (one page summary: legal name, EIN, formation date, registered agent, address types, signer list)
• Compliant address recommendation with cost, risk, and pros and cons summary
• Banking plan and application support (submitted correctly with next steps and a simple status log)
• Shopify Payments and payouts golive checklist and test order and refund confirmation
• Tracking sheet: simple compliance and admin tracker (Google Sheet) with dates, owners, and links
• Monthly retainer plan: price, what is included, and response time expectations
• Shared folder structure with clear naming and an index file:
• 01 Formation
• 02 EIN and IRS
• 03 Registered Agent and Address
• 04 Banking
• 05 Shopify Payments
• 06 Compliance Filings

WHAT WE WILL PROVIDE
• Colorado LLC documents we currently have (and EIN status)
• Owner and passport info if needed for verification
• Any existing registered agent details
• Shopify collaborator access after shortlist
• Fast responses during setup

WHO THIS IS FOR
• You have managed US LLC admin and banking onboarding before (especially with foreign owners or no SSN)
• You understand the difference between registered agent, business address, and mailing address
• You are organized, responsive, and document everything clearly
• You have supported Shopify Payments and payouts setup for US entities, and know common blockers

IMPORTANT NOTES
• We want a fully compliant setup. No fake addresses and no misrepresentation.
• We will not misrepresent our business, address, owners, or operations to banks, Shopify, or any government agency.
• If you cannot handle every part, still apply and clearly list what you can do vs what you would coordinate with a partner.

BUDGET AND TIMELINE
• Start ASAP
• Please propose a fixed price for Phase 1 and a monthly retainer for Phase
• Include estimated hours and milestone breakdown

SCREENING QUESTIONS
1) Have you supported ongoing LLC admin (not just formation) before? What tasks did you handle?

2) Based on a Colorado LLC with no US office, what address setup do you recommend for banking and Shopify verification, and why?

3) Have you helped foreign owners open a US business bank account? Which banks worked best and what were the blockers?

4) Have you set up Shopify Payments and payouts for a US LLC before? What are the most common verification issues?

5)

Pricing:
Phase 1 fixed price and estimated hours, and monthly retainer price with included scope and response time.

Contract duration of more than 6 months. with 40 hours per week.

Mandatory skills:
Administrative Support, Legal Applications & Registration, Legal Documentation, Legal Calendaring, File Documentation

jobs.description:
Job Summary:

HDFC Bank is looking for enthusiastic and dedicated individuals to join our team as Customer Officers. This role is designed for freshers who are keen to build their careers in the banking sector. As a Customer Officer, you will be the first point of contact for our customers, providing exceptional service and addressing their needs. You will play a vital role in ensuring customer satisfaction and helping the bank meet its operational goals. This is an excellent opportunity to gain experience in one of Indias leading financial institutions.
Key Responsibilities:
• * Serve as the primary point of contact for customers, providing prompt and professional service.
• * Assist customers with account-related inquiries, transactions, and banking services.
• * Process deposits, withdrawals, and other banking transactions in compliance with bank policies.
• * Resolve customer complaints and escalate complex issues when necessary.
• * Educate customers on available banking products and services, including loans, credit cards, and digital banking solutions.
• * Assist in cross-selling of banking products to maximize customer value.
• * Maintain accurate and up-to-date customer records and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
• * Collaborate with the banks internal teams to ensure seamless operations and customer satisfaction.
• Required Skills and Qualifications:
• * Bachelors degree in any discipline from a recognized university (fresh graduates encouraged to apply).
• * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
• * Strong customer service orientation with the ability to handle inquiries efficiently.
• * Basic knowledge of banking products and services is an advantage.
• * Proficiency in using computers and banking software.
• * Ability to work under pressure and handle multiple tasks effectively.
• * A professional demeanor and the ability to maintain confidentiality.
• Experience:
• * Freshers are welcome to apply. Prior experience in customer service, retail, or banking is an added advantage but not mandatory.
• Working Hours:
• * Full-time position with standard banking hours (Monday to Saturday).
• * Flexibility to work occasional weekends or extended hours based on business needs.
• Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:
• * Strong problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
• * Ability to work collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
• * Adaptability to learn quickly and stay updated on banking trends and technologies.
• * Patience, empathy, and a customer-first mindset.
• Benefits:
• * Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.
• * Health and wellness benefits, including medical insurance.
• * Professional development opportunities, including training programs and certifications.
• * Exposure to diverse roles within the bank, offering career growth and advancement.
• * A supportive and inclusive work culture.
• Why Join HDFC Bank?

Joining HDFC Bank means being a part of one of Indias largest and most reputable financial institutions. You will gain valuable experience, working in a fast-paced, customer-centric environment that prioritizes employee development. With numerous opportunities for career progression and skill enhancement, HDFC Bank is committed to nurturing talent and fostering innovation.
How to Apply:

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their updated resume along with a cover letter highlighting their qualifications and interest in the role. Applications can be submitted through the HDFC Bank career portal or via email. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the next steps in the recruitment process, including interviews and assessments.

jobs.title: Senior Digital Auditor
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary
GE is seeking a qualified professional with expertise in data privacy regulations to support global compliance initiatives. This role involves leading and conducting data privacy risk assessments, security assessments and information security audits across systems processing personally identifiable information (PII), using established frameworks to evaluate privacy and security controls, identify gaps, and prepare detailed assessment reports. The candidate will work closely with business teams to address and remediate issues, ensuring compliance with international and emerging privacy laws such as GDPR, HIPAA, DPDP, and LGPD.

Job Description

Roles and Responsibilities:
• Lead and perform data privacy assessments for systems managing personally identifiable information.
• Evaluate the design and operational effectiveness of privacy / security controls by partnering with control owners and stakeholders.
• Identify control deficiencies, support remediation efforts through resolution, and report non-conformances in a timely manner.
• Prepare and deliver comprehensive summary reports for each data privacy / security assessment or review.
• Align all activities with the Unified Control Framework (UCF) to maintain consistency and compliance across processes.
• Maintain clear and proactive communication with application teams regarding assessment scope, expectations, and timelines.
• Drive initiatives to improve processes and enhance efficiency through automation and streamlined controls.
• Educate project teams on privacy regulations and related IT control requirements to promote awareness and ensure regulatory compliance.
• Establish operating rhythm to report out on key metrics including status of assessments and issue management.
• Ensure audit readiness, provide audit support, and manage audit-related activities to facilitate successful outcomes.

Qualifications & Skills:
• Bachelor’s degree in information security, Computer Science, Information Technology, Law, or a related field.
• Some years of experience in data privacy, information security, IT audit, compliance, or risk management.
• Strong knowledge of global data privacy laws and frameworks (e.g., GDPR, LGPD, DPDP) and U.S. health data regulations (HIPAA).
• Solid understanding of the concept of personally identifiable information (PII) and its protection requirements.
• Familiarity with IT security concepts, IT controls: including access management, infrastructure, encryption, and cybersecurity practices.
• Ability to identify appropriate testing procedures and assess the effectiveness of technology and privacy controls.
• Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex issues to both technical and non-technical audiences, including engineering, legal, and project teams, focusing on risk and impact.
• Critical thinking and analytical skills to interpret data, identify trends, and assess privacy risks.
• Self-driven with the ability to independently plan and manage assessment schedules.
• Proficiency in English, both written and verbal.

Desired Characteristics
• Internationally recognized information security/information system audit certification/qualifications such as CISA, CISM, CISSP or CIA (IAPP)
• Experience with GRC tools (e.g., Archer, ServiceNow GRC).
• Experience conducting risk assessments, identifying gaps, and recommending mitigation strategies.
• Experience supporting audits and managing audit readiness activities.

Pay Range

For Poland based positions, Annual Salary Range: 213 680,00 PLN - 293 810,00 PLN

Placement Within This Range Depends On
• Relevant skills and qualifications
• Prior job-related experience
• Internal equity considerations (alignment with colleagues in similar roles) e.t.c.

We review pay ranges regularly to ensure they remain competitive with the external market and align with our internal equity considerations.

Benefits & Rewards

In addition to base salary, our employees have access to a comprehensive package of benefits and allowances, which may include:
• Health & wellness coverage
• Retirement and or savings plans
• Allowances or benefits to support role requirements (e.g., mobility, transport, or role-specific needs such as a company car or allowance where applicable)
• Work-life balance support (e.g., flexible working, leave programs)
• Recognition and incentive programs aligned with performance and company success

The exact benefits package depends on the role, location, and employment terms as specified in the Colleague Value Proposition document that will be shared prior to the interview or at the offer discussion stage.
• Performance Bonus: Details to be shared during offer discussions

Additional Information

Relocation Assistance Provided: No
