jobs.title: Clinical Data Manager Operations
jobs.description:
Are you ready for the Most Impactful Work of Your Life   

Signant Healthis a global evidence generation company   

Were helping our customers digitally enable their clinical trial programs, meeting patients where they are, driving change through technology and   

innovations and reimagining the path to proof   

Working at Signant Health puts you in the very heart of the worlds most exciting sector a high-growth, dynamic company in an extraordinary industry   

We innovate the leading-edge of clinical research data   

We do it faster, better and with an eye on the future of our industry   

Where do you fit in   

The Clinical Data Manager is responsible for working with the Clinical Data Management team and other internal project team members to provide data management services for our customers to ensure on time and accurate deliverables   

Responsibilities include providing data management guidance to internal project team members and customers; communicating with internal project team members and customers on all aspects of data management activities performed for a study; authoring data management documents; performing user acceptance testing on data management deliverables; and facilitating and verifying data clarifications and data changes   

Want to join the Best Team of Your Life   

Were looking for people who are excited by the potential to make a difference in the world   

You need to be decisive, open to celebrating mistakes, able to communicate transparently and inclusively, and someone your team can count on   

Most of all you need to be yourself   

As part of our team, your Key Accountabilities:   

Collaborate across functional groups to address technical issues impacting quality, drive forward technical initiatives within Data Management   

Provides data management guidance to internal project team members and customers communicates with internal project team members and customers on all aspects of data management activities performed for a study   

Authors data management plans (DMP) and data transfer specifications (DTS)   

Performs user acceptance testing on reports and automated data checks programmed to support the data cleaning activities specified in the DMP   

Reviews, makes decisions, and acts upon output from automated data checks   

Facilitates risk assessments for mid-study changes   

Verifies data extracts against the DTS   

Provides proactive data monitoring per contractual obligations   

Facilitates and verifies data clarifications, data corrections, and external data reconciliation   

Contribute to the development and update of process improvements that reduce processing times, increase quality, mitigate operations risks, improve team performance, and meet departmental objectives and corporate objectives communicates effectively within a multi-disciplinary project team to complete assigned tasks on time and within budget   

Assists in developing and improving Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Working Instructions and Best Practices   

Youll need to bring:-   

BA/BS degree in a relevant field (Medicine, Statistics, Computer Science) or equivalent work experience   

Ability to complete high quality technical documentation and to understand requirements related to system and front-end functionality, including how that impacts how and when data is saved into the database   

Proven ability to work both independently and in a team-oriented environment, providing back-up support to team members & establishing/maintaining effective work relationships with co-workers within and across functional areas   

Previously demonstrated proactive and positive approach totasks andprojects overall,as well as to the types ofscheduling & process changes that are inherent in a fast-paced business   

be thrilled to hear that you also have:   

5+ years of professional experience in the clinical trials data management field   

Knowledge of SAS   

Experience in providing data management services in the clinical trials industry   

Proficient in Access, Excel, and other office software technologies and applications   

Proven ability to handle multiple competing priorities in a fast-paced work environment with minimal direct supervision   

Familiarity with 21 CFR Part 11 or experience in a regulated environment desirable   

What youll need to succeed with us   

Strong verbal and written communication skills   

Demonstrate extreme attention to detail and organization in all aspects of work   

Ability to quickly learn and apply new skills, procedures, and approaches   

Demonstrated ability to meet very short deadlines & multi-task in an extremely fast-paced work environment with little direct supervision   

Decision Making and Influence:   

Participates In Special Data-Related Projects As Needed   

Assist With Other Departmental Initiatives And Projects As Required   

Mentors Less Experience Staff   

Ensure Project Staff Are Trained And Adhere To Project-Specific, Global, Standardized Data Management Processes   

Represent Clinical Data Management In Client Visits, Audits, Inspections And Bid Defences As Needed   

We know that everyone has different wants and needs, which is why along with a highly competitive base salary we support our people and their loved ones with a variety of perks and benefits   

As part of our team some of the benefits you can expect to receive are:   

Medical Insurance, Group Accidental Coverage/Insurance, Group Term Life Insurance company Paid Subscription to Calm The #1 app for mental fitness   

Employee Referral Program Bring the Best to Signant Health and earn a reward   

Wellness Program Participate in challenges and earn points for rewards   

Proof Signants Employee Recognition Program where you can accumulate points to redeem for exciting merchandise, gift cards, tickets, and more   

BurnAlong Digital fitness and wellness platform   

Does this sound like something youd like to exploreThen love to hear from you   

To apply, please submit your CV and a cover letter letting us know why you think youd be perfect for this role   

We will begin reviewing submissions during the application period and will fill the vacancy as soon as a suitable candidate is identified   

At Signant Health, accepting difference isnt enoughwe celebrate it, we support it, and we nurture it for the benefit of our team members, our clients and our community   

Signant Health is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer   

We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or veteran status   

Apply Now

jobs.title: Manager – Administration
jobs.description:
Job Title:
 Manager – Administration & Operations

Location:
 Hyderabad, India

Organization:
 TiE Hyderabad (The Indus Entrepreneurs, Hyderabad Chapter)

Employment Type:
 Full-time

We are seeking a dependable and detail-oriented Manager – Administration & Operations to manage day-to-day office operations, vendor coordination, financial administration, and compliance.

 The role is critical to ensuring smooth internal functioning and operational efficiency of the organization.

Key Responsibilities

Office Administration & OperationsOversee day-to-day office operations and ensure smooth functioning of the workplace.
Manage office facilities, maintenance, utilities, housekeeping, security, and infrastructure.
Maintain inventory of office supplies, equipment, and assets; ensure timely procurement and replenishment.
Support logistics and operational arrangements for internal meetings and organizational activities.


Procurement & Vendor ManagementIdentify, onboard, and manage vendors for office services, printing, events, IT, and other operational needs.
Obtain quotations, negotiate commercial terms, and process purchase orders.
Track vendor deliverables and ensure timely service completion.
Manage invoice verification, documentation, and submission for payment processing.


Finance & Accounts Coordination
Coordinate with the accounts/finance function for:
Invoice processing and payment scheduling
Vendor payment follow-ups
Expense tracking and documentation
Budget monitoring for administrative expenses
Maintain records of contracts, invoices, payments, and financial documentation.
Support preparation of financial data for audits and internal reporting.


Compliance & DocumentationEnsure adherence to statutory and regulatory requirements applicable to a non-profit organization (GST, TDS, professional tax, etc.) in coordination with external consultants.

Maintain organizational records, agreements, MoUs, and compliance documentation.
Support timely submission of statutory filings and audit requirements.
Ensure compliance with internal policies, procurement processes, and approval workflows.


Process Improvement & Operational SupportEstablish and streamline administrative processes and standard operating procedures (SOPs).
Support leadership with operational planning, vendor evaluations, and cost optimization.
Provide administrative support for organizational initiatives and special projects as required.

Requirements
5–10 years of experience in administration, operations, office management, or finance coordination roles.
Experience handling vendor management, procurement, and office operations.
Working knowledge of basic accounting processes, invoice management, and financial documentation.
Familiarity with statutory compliance and audit coordination (preferably for NGOs, associations, or small organizations).
Strong organizational, negotiation, and problem-solving skills.
Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and basic accounting/ERP tools.
High level of integrity, ownership, and attention to detail.

Preferred Attributes
Experience working in a non-profit, startup, or small team environment.
Ability to manage multiple responsibilities independently with minimal supervision.
Strong coordination and stakeholder management skills.
Cost-conscious approach with focus on operational efficiency.

Why Join TiE Hyderabad?
Opportunity to work at the heart of Hyderabad's entrepreneurial ecosystem.
Exposure to industry leaders, entrepreneurs, and ecosystem partners.
Dynamic and collaborative work environment with strong learning opportunities.
Be part of a globally recognized network supporting innovation and entrepreneurship.

How to Apply
Interested candidates may send their resume to  with the subject line:
Application – Manager (Administration & Operations)

About TiE Hyderabad ChapterTiE (The Indus Entrepreneurs) Hyderabad Chapter is a go-to network of 180+ successful Charter Members & 360+ Associate members and a vibrant and most recognized chapter worldwide.

 Each year, TiE Hyderabad impacts over 1500 startups and 6400+ students, and conducts 120+ Programs, events & activities under 20+ themes, viz.

 TiE Open Mic, TiE Scale, Mentor Advisor, Investor Connect, & Leadership Series, TiE Grad, TiE Young Entrepreneurs, nurture next-generation entrepreneurs.

 TiE organization comprises of successful entrepreneurs & accomplished professionals, volunteering to support startups, with an aim of fostering entrepreneurship across industries, at every stage of the entrepreneurial life cycle.

 Handholding start-up founders & entrepreneurs to the next level of growth through mentoring, education, funding & connecting customers & student initiatives in a big way.

 TiE Hyderabad works extensively with founders, investors, corporates, academia, government, and ecosystem enablers to strengthen the region's entrepreneurial fabric.

jobs.title: SAP BTP Platform Operations Manager IS&T SAP
jobs.description:
Responsibilities    

SAP BTP Platform Administration, Troubleshooting, Maintenance, and Change Management and tasks;  play a crucial role in ensuring the smooth operation of critical production workloads. 

 Managing key stakeholders and conveying the business leaders about the critical health of business applications. 

 Act as an escalation point for complex technical issues and provide expert guidance to the team. 

 Oversee the end-to-end administration of the SAP BTP environment, including provisioning, configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting of various BTP services (e.g., Cloud Foundry, Kyma Runtime, Integration Suite, Analytics Cloud, Process Automation, Data Intelligence, etc.). 

 Ensure the high availability, performance, scalability, and security of all BTP landscapes and deployed applications. 

 Develop and implement robust monitoring, alerting, and logging strategies for BTP services and applications. 

 Collaborate closely with application development teams, enterprise architects, security teams, and business stakeholders to understand requirements and provide technical guidance. 

 Communicate platform status, issues, and strategic direction to senior management and relevant teams. 

 Drive continuous improvement initiatives for BTP operations, automation, and service delivery. 

 Global Traffic Management & Security: Design and implement robust security enforcement policies within SAP APIM using OAuth, JWT, MTLS, and SAML. This includes configuring complex principal propagation scenarios to ensure identity is preserved across multi-cloud hops. 

 Enterprise-Grade Event Mesh: Architect high-performance, asynchronous communication models using SAP Advanced Event Mesh. You must be able to design publisher-subscriber patterns that integrate seamlessly with SAP BTP Integration Suite to enable real-time, scalable data streaming. Also, should have expertise in managing DR & DMR links. 

 Hybrid Landscape Connectivity: Manage and optimize connectivity across diverse environments using Edge Integration Cell and Cloud Connector to bridge on-premise legacy systems with cloud-native services. 

 Infrastructure & Reliability: Set up and manage SAP APIM across multiple non-Production and Production subaccounts, implementing Custom Domains and disaster recovery configurations to ensure system stability. 

 Advanced Event Mesh Implementation: Architect complex event-driven messaging solutions that support high-concurrency and sub-millisecond latency requirements. 

 Orchestration & Integration: Leverage SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) in conjunction with AEM to create decoupled, responsive systems that integrate SAP ECC with non-SAP systems. 

 Performance Validation: Conduct rigorous API testing and performance validation using to ensure the reliability of mission-critical data flows. 

 Technical Expertise & Qualifications 

 Minimum Qualifications 

 12+ years of Overall Experience and and 5+ years on SAP BTP. 

 3-5 years in leading SAP platform operations, cloud operations, or enterprise middleware/platform engineering. 

 2+ years of People Management experience and strong StakeHolder management and Escalation management  experience. 

 Experience in SAP BTP Platform Administration and Integration Suite. 

 Strong expertise in BTP platform security and authentication mechanisms (OAuth2, XSUAA, JWT, SAML, IAS, certificate-based auth, trust configurations) 

 Proven experience in IT operations, incident/change management, and platform reliability. 

 Excellent debugging and troubleshooting skills, perform deep root-cause analysis across application, platform, security, and network layers 

 A strong sense of ownership, good critical thinking & interpersonal skills to work successfully across diverse business and technical & multi-functional teams. 

 Preferred Qualifications 

 Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Engineering or other relevant major. 

 SAP BTP / Cloud / Basis certification (preferred but not mandatory) 

 Solid skills in cloud networking concepts (VPC/VNet, routing, DNS, load balancing, firewalls, private endpoints, hybrid connectivity) 

 Proven ability to lead cross-functional technical operations teams and manage escalations 

 Strong expertise in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration, API Management, Advanced Event Mesh) operations and support

jobs.title: Air Logistic Sales Representative - (Ahmedabad & Baroda)
jobs.description:
It's more than a job

With a sales career at Kuehne+Nagel, you'll drive long-term business success by mastering sales strategies, nurturing customer relationships and identifying new opportunities. But you will also bring relief and peace of mind to pet owners around the world. Because when you do your job, your colleagues around the world are able to build reliable veterinary supply chains that ensure pets get the care they need. At Kuehne+Nagel, our work is about more than we imagine.

‎

You will be part of our customer care team to add your expertise + skills to the delivery of customer excellence. You will be utilizing your product + trade knowledge to support business development + diversification to drive business growth.

How you create impact
• To take lead in monitoring + driving sales activities.
• To proactively identify + nurturing sales-qualified prospects through the pipeline to generate profitable + sustainable businesses.
• To conduct regular reviews of sales leads + customer portfolios with key stakeholders.
• To conduct market research on pricing + product competitiveness, ensure regular efforts alignment is being make with our pricing team.
• To provide CCL team the customer’s feedback + support on resolutions as required.
• To take the lead in onboarding + implementation of new customer, ensuring that standard operational procedures are update for handing over to our Customer Care Locations (CCL).
• To maintain + update relevant customer information on internal systems.
• To focus on customer engagement, satisfaction, retention + reactivation through high levels of service quality in close collaboration with the CCL.

What we would like you to bring
• Minimum 4 - 5 years work experience in sales, particularly in the freight forwarding industry.
• The preferred candidate should be proficient in spoken Marathi and must have a strong local customer base.
• Must have a strong local customer base and market intelligence.
• Strong customer service skills to build and maintain client relationships.
• Excellent communication, professional selling and problem-solving abilities.
• Ability to work well within a team and support colleagues as needed.

What's in it for you

At Kuehne+Nagel, you’ll be part of a global logistics leader that believes in creating real impact—on business, on customers, and on careers. Here’s what you can look forward to:
• Global Exposure: Step into a world of international opportunities with a presence in 100+ countries.
• People-Centric Culture: Join a team where your voice matters and people genuinely care.
• Learning & Development: Grow with us—personally and professionally—through world-class training and career pathways.
• Innovation & Sustainability: Be part of a future-focused company driving real change in logistics and the planet.
• Rewards & Recognition: Get rewarded for your passion, performance, and potential.
• Stability with Agility: Enjoy the best of both worlds—a trusted global brand with a startup spirit.

Who we are

Logistics shapes everyday life - from the goods we consume to the healthcare we rely on. At Kuehne+Nagel, your work goes beyond logistics; it enables both ordinary and special moments in the lives of people around the world.

As a global leader with a strong heritage and a vision to move the world forward, we offer a safe, stable environment where your career can make a real difference. Whether we help deliver life-saving medicines, develop sustainable transportation solutions or support our local communities, your career will contribute to more than you can imagine.

jobs.title: Air Operations Associate, Gurgaon
jobs.description:
About Flexport:

At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes - from emerging brands to Fortune 500s - use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.

The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.

Help our customers improve their global supply chains

The Opportunity:

As an Air Operations Associate, you’ll play a key role in ensuring smooth and timely air freight movements for our customers. You’ll coordinate daily with airlines, trucking partners, terminals, and internal teams to keep shipments on track.

This role combines hands-on operations, customer service, and documentation work - helping deliver seamless supply chain execution while driving efficiency, volume growth, and profitability.

What You’ll Do:
• Execute end-to-end air shipments :
• Receiving booking, contact with shipper/consignee and agent to arrange the shipment within SOP’s provided.
• Input data into system to create file, completing Mawb and Hawbs, ensure correct & on time handling of files, file AMS or submit EDI (FHL/FWB) as required
• Timely invoicing to clients/ agents as well as checking and confirming incoming invoices of sub-contractors on time & correctly.
• Customer service by following up on customer advices/request or claim and feedback to customer and overseas and make respective shipment arrangement
• Inform senior/manager if any irregularities or problems that require assistance and to feedback on subcontractors’s standards and services.
• Ensure all operational activities comply with international shipping regulations, company policies, and customer SOPs.
• Handle customers statistics and reports or any special request for improving the system.
• Support data and system accuracy: Keep Flexport systems updated to meet KPIs and maintain high-quality data.
• Collaborate across teams: Work closely with account managers and customs teams to ensure smooth operations and a great customer experience.
• Drive improvement: Identify and suggest process enhancements to increase efficiency and quality.
• Take ownership: Support teammates and take on additional tasks when needed/ required.

What We’re Looking For:
• Bachelor’s degree in logistics, supply chain, business, or related fields.
• 1–2 years of experience in air operation or related roles in freight forwarding/logistics.
• IATA Certification
• Good communication of written and spoken English.
• Detail-oriented, structured, and eager to learn global supply chain operations.
• Customer-focused mindset: respond promptly and professionally to customer needs, answering calls and dealing with customer queries, providing first-class customer service at all times.

What You’ll Gain:
• Deep expertise in air freight operations (imports, exports, intermodal).
• Hands-on exposure to international logistics systems (carriers, customs, warehouses).
• Opportunity to grow into Senior Associate, Team Leader, or Account Management roles.
• Be part of a fast-growing international company with training and development from global experts.

Commitment to Equal Opportunity

At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, health status, marital and family status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants

By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. Please see our Privacy Notice available at www.flexport.com/privacy for additional information.

jobs.title: Logistics Operations Specialist 1
jobs.description:
Summary Of The Role

The Senior Logistics Operations Specialist will serve as the single point of contact for Supply Chain Operations in Dahej, Gujrat, including warehousing and inbound/outbound transportation activities. This role ensures seamless coordination across all operational areas including inventory control, material movement, and compliance with safety and quality standards.

The position will lead the entire warehouse operation, providing guidance and support to Logistics Operation Specialists. Strong operational leadership, stakeholder management, and process improvement skills are essential to drive efficiency and achieve business objectives.

⸻

Key Responsibilities
• Warehouse Operations Supervision
• Serve as the single point of contact for all operational deliverables, issue resolution, and overall SCM operations at Dahej.
• Manage daily warehouse activities, including inbound and outbound shipments, ensuring timely and accurate processing.
• Optimize workflows to improve productivity and reduce operational costs.
• Collaborate closely with the Warehouse Operations Manager and cross-functional teams to ensure smooth execution of all activities.
• Team Coordination
• Provide guidance and mentorship to Logistics Operation Specialists
• Support manpower planning, shift scheduling, and workload balancing.
• Foster collaboration and knowledge sharing across the team.
• 3PL Management
• Manage relationships with third-party logistics (3PL) providers, ensuring contract compliance and service excellence.
• Monitor 3PL performance metrics such as order fulfillment, transportation, and warehousing efficiency.
• Resolve operational issues with 3PL partners promptly and effectively.
• Collaborate with 3PL providers to implement best practices and cost-saving initiatives.
• Inventory Control & Management
• Maintain 100% inventory accuracy through robust controls and timely updates.
• Conduct regular inventory audits and cycle counts to maintain accuracy.
• Monitor stock levels and coordinate replenishment to avoid shortages or excess.
• Implement measures to minimize shrinkage, damage, and waste.
• Safety & Compliance
• Ensure adherence to workplace safety regulations and company policies.
• Conduct regular inspections to maintain a clean, hazard-free environment.
• Train staff in safety protocols and proper equipment handling.
• Comply with all regulatory requirements related to warehousing and logistics.
• Process Improvement & Reporting
• Identify operational gaps and implement process improvements for efficiency and cost reduction.
• Maintain accurate records of warehouse activities, including shipments, returns, and damages.
• Generate and analyze reports on inventory levels, order fulfillment rates, and operational KPIs.

Requirements

Education & Certification
• Diploma or Bachelor’s degree required; Supply Chain or Logistics specialization preferred.
• Professional certifications in warehouse or supply chain management are an advantage.

Experience & Skills
• Minimum 7–10 years of experience in warehouse operations, including at least 3 years in a supervisory role managing blue-collar teams
• Proven experience in manpower planning and team coordination.
• Strong knowledge of warehouse processes, inventory control, and 3PL management.
• Proficiency in SAP and MS Office tools.
• Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment with strong organizational and prioritization skills.
• Excellent interpersonal and communication skills for cross-functional collaboration.

Key Competencies
• Analytical and Problem-Solving Skills
• Result Orientation
• Adaptability and Change Management
• Building Trust and Collaboration
• Continuous Improvement Mindset

Experience managing multi-location projects & warehouses

Success Metrics (KPIs)
• Zero execution delays due to procurement

• Strong vendor performance scores

jobs.title: Head Supply Chain
jobs.description:
Please send your CV to

Head Supply Chain (CXO Role - Agri background is must)
Also designated as Lead – Supply Chain

Location:
Dawalwadi (near Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Maharashtra)

Reports to:

CEO
Position Summary

We are seeking for a client a dynamic and forward-thinking Supply Chain & Operations Leader to drive Company's end-to-end supply chain and manufacturing operations.

This role will oversee seed processing, packing, storage, warehousing, and logistics, ensuring timely and quality delivery across the value chain.
The ideal candidate will combine operational excellence with strong business acumen to ensure strategic goals are delivered.

Key Responsibilities

Operational & Supply Chain Leadership
Own and drive the complete supply chain function, from seed processing and packaging to warehousing and last-mile logistics.
Ensure seamless coordination between manufacturing units, third-party tollers, and distribution partners.
Maintain high seed quality standards and ensure zero compromise across processing, packaging, and storage.
Optimize warehousing infrastructure and streamline inventory management for reduced wastage and cost efficiency.
Strategic & Financial Oversight
Develop and execute a cost-efficient supply chain strategy aligned with Mahyco's business objectives.
Deliver on key operational KPIs, while ensuring budgetary control and P&L accountability for the function.
Leverage digital tools and automation to modernize supply chain operations and enhance traceability and responsiveness.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Act as a key bridge between Production, R&D, Sales, Marketing, and Finance to ensure business continuity and supply alignment with market demand.
Support product launches and seasonal planning through agile supply planning and coordination.
Team Building & Leadership

Build and lead a high-performance team across manufacturing, logistics, and warehousing.
Mentor and develop future functional leaders and instill a culture of ownership, agility, and excellence.

Compliance & Stakeholder Management
Ensure full compliance with regulatory standards related to seed processing, storage, and transport.
Build strong working relationships with government, tolling partners, and industry stakeholders to stay ahead of regulatory and policy trends.

Ideal Candidate Profile

Experience: 15+ years of experience in agri-inputs (preferably seed), food processing, or a related supply chain-intensive industry. At least 5 years in a senior leadership role with end-to-end supply chain responsibility.

Education:
Science background (Post graduate in agriculture/biology/engineering); a postgraduate degree in Supply Chain, Operations, or Business Management is preferred.

Industry Exposure:
Proven understanding of the agri-input or seed business; experience with best-in-class operations processes and digital tools.

Key Competencies
• Strong business and P&L orientation.
• Strategic thinker with hands-on execution ability.
• Excellent team-building and people leadership skills.
• Analytical and data-driven decision-making mindset.
• High integrity, agility, and resilience in managing seasonal and scale-sensitive operations.
Please send your CV to

jobs.title: Supply Network Planning Sr Analyst
jobs.description:
OverviewThis role supports the development of the next generation network design capability, enabling industry leading design capability for all Global business units, Sectors, and ND Global Capability Centers, across the End-to-End Value Chain.

 This role is responsible for analyzing and interpreting data to provide actionable insights that improve the efficiency and performance of the supply chain network while also supporting long-term growth. This is accomplished by partnering with the Global Network Design Modelers and stakeholders focusing on data-driven decision making, reporting, and supporting model development while testing new state-of-the-art optimization software.

 This will help enable identification of transformative projects that will deliver significant levels of profitability, efficiency, sustainability, and service, that align with the organization’s global operational objectives.

 Responsibilities Collect, clean and analyse data to support network optimization projects Run scenario analysis and sensitivities using new and existing optimization tools Identify trends and bottlenecks in supply chain operations through data visualization Assist GND modeler in model validation and calibration using real-world data Collaborate with GND Modelers to test and refine optimization solutions Support ad-hoc analytics requests from business teams Develop dashboards and reports to communicate insights to stakeholders Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities concurrently and work well under tight timelines Develops domain knowledge and Subject Matter Expertise (SME) in Network Modeling and analytics  Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in operations research, Industrial Engineering/Supply Chain or similar Strong analytical skills with experience in Excel, SQL, and data visualization tools like Power, BI or Tableau Ability to handle large datasets and draw meaningful insights from them  Skills

  Leadership Strong Communicator Results Oriented Agility to solve problems Analytical Thinking Teamwork Excellent interpersonal relationships Proficiency in Microsoft office suite, Tableau and Power BI Manage of big data bases on SQL or similar Use of Supply Chain Guru and Data Guru (nice to have)

 The ideal candidate will combine operational excellence with strong business acumen to ensure strategic goals are delivered.

Key Responsibilities

Operational & Supply Chain Leadership
Own and drive the complete supply chain function, from seed processing and packaging to warehousing and last-mile logistics.
Ensure seamless coordination between manufacturing units, third-party tollers, and distribution partners.
Maintain high seed quality standards and ensure zero compromise across processing, packaging, and storage.
Optimize warehousing infrastructure and streamline inventory management for reduced wastage and cost efficiency.
Strategic & Financial Oversight
Develop and execute a cost-efficient supply chain strategy aligned with Mahyco's business objectives.
Deliver on key operational KPIs, while ensuring budgetary control and P&L accountability for the function.
Leverage digital tools and automation to modernize supply chain operations and enhance traceability and responsiveness.

Cross-Functional CollaborationAct as a key bridge between Production, R&D, Sales, Marketing, and Finance to ensure business continuity and supply alignment with market demand.

Support product launches and seasonal planning through agile supply planning and coordination.
Team Building & Leadership

Build and lead a high-performance team across manufacturing, logistics, and warehousing.
Mentor and develop future functional leaders and instill a culture of ownership, agility, and excellence.

Compliance & Stakeholder Management
Ensure full compliance with regulatory standards related to seed processing, storage, and transport.
Build strong working relationships with government, tolling partners, and industry stakeholders to stay ahead of regulatory and policy trends.

Ideal Candidate Profile

Experience: 15+ years of experience in agri-inputs (preferably seed), food processing, or a related supply chain-intensive industry. At least 5 years in a senior leadership role with end-to-end supply chain responsibility.

Education:
 Science background (Post graduate in agriculture/biology/engineering); a postgraduate degree in Supply Chain, Operations, or Business Management is preferred.

Industry Exposure:
 Proven understanding of the agri-input or seed business; experience with best-in-class operations processes and digital tools.

Key Competencies


 Strong business and P&L orientation. Strategic thinker with hands-on execution ability. Excellent team-building and people leadership skills. Analytical and data-driven decision-making mindset. High integrity, agility, and resilience in managing seasonal and scale-sensitive operations.Please send your CV to

jobs.title: Air Operations Associate, Gurgaon
jobs.description:
About Flexport: 

At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes - from emerging brands to Fortune 500s - use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.

The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.

Help our customers improve their global supply chains

The Opportunity:

As an Air Operations Associate, you’ll play a key role in ensuring smooth and timely air freight movements for our customers. You’ll coordinate daily with airlines, trucking partners, terminals, and internal teams to keep shipments on track.

This role combines hands-on operations, customer service, and documentation work - helping deliver seamless supply chain execution while driving efficiency, volume growth, and profitability.

What You’ll Do: 

Execute end-to-end air shipments :Receiving booking, contact with shipper/consignee and agent to arrange the shipment within SOP’s provided. Input data into system to create file, completing Mawb and Hawbs, ensure correct & on time handling of files, file AMS or submit EDI (FHL/FWB) as requiredTimely invoicing to clients/ agents as well as checking and confirming incoming invoices of sub-contractors on time & correctly. Customer service by following up on customer advices/request or claim and feedback to customer and overseas and make respective shipment arrangementInform senior/manager if any irregularities or problems that require assistance and to feedback on subcontractors’s standards and services. Ensure all operational activities comply with international shipping regulations, company policies, and customer SOPs. Handle customers statistics and reports or any special request for improving the system. Support data and system accuracy: Keep Flexport systems updated to meet KPIs and maintain high-quality data. Collaborate across teams: Work closely with account managers and customs teams to ensure smooth operations and a great customer experience. Drive improvement: Identify and suggest process enhancements to increase efficiency and quality. Take ownership: Support teammates and take on additional tasks when needed/ required. 

What We’re Looking For:

Bachelor’s degree in logistics, supply chain, business, or related fields. 1–2 years of experience in air operation or related roles in freight forwarding/logistics. IATA CertificationGood communication of written and spoken English. Detail-oriented, structured, and eager to learn global supply chain operations. Customer-focused mindset: respond promptly and professionally to customer needs, answering calls and dealing with customer queries, providing first-class customer service at all times. 

What You’ll Gain:

Deep expertise in air freight operations (imports, exports, intermodal). Hands-on exposure to international logistics systems (carriers, customs, warehouses). Opportunity to grow into Senior Associate, Team Leader, or Account Management roles. Be part of a fast-growing international company with training and development from global experts. 

Commitment to Equal Opportunity

At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, health status, marital and family status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants

Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants. By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. Please see our Privacy Notice available at www.flexport.com/privacy for additional information.

jobs.title: Logistics Operations Specialist 1
jobs.description:
Summary Of The Role

The Senior Logistics Operations Specialist will serve as the single point of contact for Supply Chain Operations in Dahej, Gujrat, including warehousing and inbound/outbound transportation activities. This role ensures seamless coordination across all operational areas including inventory control, material movement, and compliance with safety and quality standards.

The position will lead the entire warehouse operation, providing guidance and support to Logistics Operation Specialists. Strong operational leadership, stakeholder management, and process improvement skills are essential to drive efficiency and achieve business objectives.

⸻

Key Responsibilities

 Warehouse Operations SupervisionServe as the single point of contact for all operational deliverables, issue resolution, and overall SCM operations at Dahej.Manage daily warehouse activities, including inbound and outbound shipments, ensuring timely and accurate processing.Optimize workflows to improve productivity and reduce operational costs.Collaborate closely with the Warehouse Operations Manager and cross-functional teams to ensure smooth execution of all activities. Team CoordinationProvide guidance and mentorship to Logistics Operation SpecialistsSupport manpower planning, shift scheduling, and workload balancing.Foster collaboration and knowledge sharing across the team. 3PL ManagementManage relationships with third-party logistics (3PL) providers, ensuring contract compliance and service excellence.Monitor 3PL performance metrics such as order fulfillment, transportation, and warehousing efficiency.Resolve operational issues with 3PL partners promptly and effectively.Collaborate with 3PL providers to implement best practices and cost-saving initiatives. Inventory Control & ManagementMaintain 100% inventory accuracy through robust controls and timely updates.Conduct regular inventory audits and cycle counts to maintain accuracy.Monitor stock levels and coordinate replenishment to avoid shortages or excess.Implement measures to minimize shrinkage, damage, and waste. Safety & ComplianceEnsure adherence to workplace safety regulations and company policies.Conduct regular inspections to maintain a clean, hazard-free environment.Train staff in safety protocols and proper equipment handling.Comply with all regulatory requirements related to warehousing and logistics. Process Improvement & ReportingIdentify operational gaps and implement process improvements for efficiency and cost reduction.Maintain accurate records of warehouse activities, including shipments, returns, and damages.Generate and analyze reports on inventory levels, order fulfillment rates, and operational KPIs.

Requirements

Education & Certification

Diploma or Bachelor’s degree required; Supply Chain or Logistics specialization preferred.Professional certifications in warehouse or supply chain management are an advantage.

Experience & Skills

Minimum 7–10 years of experience in warehouse operations, including at least 3 years in a supervisory role managing blue-collar teamsProven experience in manpower planning and team coordination.Strong knowledge of warehouse processes, inventory control, and 3PL management.Proficiency in SAP and MS Office tools.Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment with strong organizational and prioritization skills.Excellent interpersonal and communication skills for cross-functional collaboration.

Key Competencies

Analytical and Problem-Solving SkillsResult OrientationAdaptability and Change ManagementBuilding Trust and CollaborationContinuous Improvement Mindset

Proven examples of posts that performed well 
Familiarity with creator-style editing beats (open loops, resets, callbacks)

jobs.title: Principal Corporate Counsel- Telecom, Market access 10+yrs exp
jobs.description:
Meet the Team

The Regulatory Affairs & Compliance team within Cisco’s Legal department is comprised of lawyers, program managers, operational specialists, engineers, and subject matter experts focused on topics including compliance and ethics (anti-bribery and anti-corruption), global trade, cybersecurity, telecom, environmental sustainability, and various other regulatory practice areas and programs. We partner with colleagues in Legal and functional teams across the company to drive compliance and impact.

The team is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Principal Corporate Counsel to advise on telecommunications regulations, lawful intercept laws and other market access regulatory issues in the Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC) Region. You will collaborate with a team of legal professionals providing guidance and support on market access matters including compliance counseling to support product launches.

Your Impact
• Provide strategic legal advice and support on matters related to telecommunications and lawful intercept laws, including regulatory product compliance and licensing.
• Develop policies, guidelines, and procedures to promote compliance with regulatory requirements.
• Advise teams on regulatory aspects of product development and launches.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including engineering and sales, to implement compliance strategies that enable business objectives.
• Partner with Cisco’s Government Affairs team to help shape evolving regulations.
• Recommend pragmatic solutions to meet regulatory requirements and address potential compliance issues.

Minimum Qualifications
• Juris Doctor (J.D.) degree or equivalent from an accredited law school.
• Licensed attorney in good standing in one or more jurisdictions where Cisco does business.
• 10+ years of relevant in-house legal and/or law firm experience.
• Demonstrated in-depth knowledge and understanding of telecommunications regulations and familiarity with lawful intercept laws.
• Strong experience working collaboratively with cross-functional teams to develop, deliver, and guide effective, efficient regulatory compliance strategies and programs.
• Excellent presentation and written communication skills to both business and legal audiences.
• Experience working with regulatory bodies and industry groups.

Preferred Qualifications
• Experience working in a large technology or telecommunications company or similar industry.
• Experience exploring AI applications to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and the ability to predict and mitigate compliance risks.

Why Cisco?

At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.

Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere.

We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

jobs.title: LinkedIn Page Manager for Law Firm
jobs.description:
We are a Mauritius-based boutique law firm specialising in cross-border corporate, finance and disputes work across Africa.

We are looking for a reliable, thoughtful LinkedIn content manager to help grow our firm's visibility and reach among:

CEOs and business leaders

Financial services professionals

Corporate advisers

International investors using Mauritius structures

This is not high-volume social media work. It is targeted, professional, and credibility-driven.

Scope of Work

You will:
Draft and schedule 1–2 LinkedIn posts per week

Repurpose:
Articles we publish on our website

Events we host (1–2 per quarter)

Law firm rankings (Chambers & Partners, IFLR1000, Legal 500)

Occasional short event videos

Suggest positioning angles to make content engaging and strategic

Ensure tone aligns with high-end professional services branding

Optimise formatting for LinkedIn (hooks, structure, readability)

Suggest light engagement strategies (polls, questions, thought-leadership positioning where relevant)

We will provide:
Raw content (articles, event photos, videos)

Core messages

Background on positioning and strategy

What We're Looking For

Experience managing LinkedIn for:
Law firms, consulting firms, accounting firms, investment firms, or other professional services

Strong B2B writing skills (clear, structured, intelligent tone)

Ability to simplify complex topics without dumbing them down

Understanding of LinkedIn algorithm and formatting best practices

Discretion and professionalism

Bonus:
Familiarity with African markets

Experience working with legal or financial content

Tone & Positioning

Our content should feel:
Intelligent but accessible

Strategic, not self-promotional

Confident, not boastful

Commercially aware

Africa-facing and internationally credible

We are not looking for gimmicky marketing.

Expected Time Commitment

Approx. 4–6 hours per week.

Ongoing engagement preferred (3–6 months initially).

To Apply

Please share:
2–3 LinkedIn posts you have written for professional services firms

Your approach to increasing reach without compromising credibility

Your monthly fee for 1–2 posts per week

Contract duration of 1 to 3 months. with 40 hours per week.

Mandatory skills:
Content Writing, Social Media Management, Social Media Marketing, Writing, Copywriting

jobs.title: CIC SSE SAM Specialist L2
jobs.description:
Role Overview:
As a SAM Specialist at our company, you will be responsible for managing software assets, ensuring license compliance, optimizing software spend, and maintaining process excellence. With your expertise in SAM processes and hands-on experience with ServiceNow SAM Pro Implementation, you will play a crucial role in effective lifecycle management of software assets.

Key Responsibilities:
- Manage software discovery, normalization, and reconciliation.
- Process incoming license requests, validate user eligibility, manage approval workflows, and track available license pool.
- Ensure proper removal of licenses and applications from user/devices.
- Track software usage entitlements and compliance organization-wide.
- Prepare periodic BAU reports and conduct license compliance audits for major vendors.
- Implement software harvesting, re-harvesting, and license optimization strategies.
- Identify opportunities to reduce software costs and improve license utilization.
- Assist in the end-to-end implementation of ServiceNow SAM Pro for key publishers.
- Configure software models, publisher packs, normalization rules, and entitlement mappings.
- Participate in loading and validating license entitlements, contracts, SKUs, and product-use rights.
- Configure and test reconciliation rules for compliance positions.
- Build dashboards and reports for usage compliance and optimization.
- Manage integration between ServiceNow and Intune for software discovery and usage data.

jobs.title: Bubble Developer for DOT Compliance SaaS
jobs.description:
ClearRoute Compliance is a SaaS web application designed to help trucking businesses (owner-operators and small to mid-sized fleets) manage DOT compliance requirements in a simple, organized dashboard.

The app will allow users to sign up, onboard as either an Owner-Operator or Fleet, track compliance checklist items, upload and manage required documents with expiration dates, and receive automated reminders.
The goal is to keep users audit-ready without the complexity of manual paperwork. Stripe subscription billing will be integrated.

Key features / pages
• Landing + Pricing
• Signup / Login
• Onboarding (choose Owner-Operator vs Fleet)

Collect:
company name, DOT/MC, authority start date, fleet size (if fleet), contact info
• Dashboard
• Audit-ready status (Ready / Not Ready)
• Counts of missing/due soon/complete items
• Upcoming deadlines
• Compliance Checklist
• Master checklist items + per-company status (red/yellow/green)
• Due dates + completion tracking + notes
• Documents Vault
• Upload/store docs by category
• Track expiration dates
• Link docs to checklist items where applicable
• Reminders
• Email reminders for upcoming due dates and expiring documents (Phase 1)
• Billing
• Stripe subscription (Owner-Op plan + Fleet tiered plans)
• Manage subscription / cancel
• Support / FAQ + Contact
Key actions users will perform
• Create account and select Owner-Op or Fleet
• Enter company/DOT authority info
• View dashboard audit status and upcoming deadlines
• Mark checklist items complete / update due dates where needed
• Upload compliance documents and add expiry dates
• Receive reminder emails and take action (upload/renew/complete)
• Subscribe and manage billing (Stripe)
Integrations
• Stripe for subscription billing (test mode first, then live)

Email delivery:
Bubble native email initially; can upgrade to SendGrid later for deliverability
• (Optional later) FMCSA/SMS or DOT data lookup via API if feasible; not required for MVP
Examples of similar apps (functionally)
• Compliance/document tracking dashboards like Gusto / Rippling style admin checklists (but for trucking)
• Fleet compliance management tools; MVP focus is checklist + document vault + reminders + billing
Notes / scope guidance
• MVP priority is solid data model, privacy rules, and reliable checklist/document workflow.
• UI should be clean and mobile-responsive, but perfection can be Phase 2.
SaaS web app for owner-operators and fleets to manage DOT compliance. Includes onboarding, checklist tracking, document uploads with expiry dates, reminders, and Stripe subscriptions.

Contract duration of 1 to 3 months.

Mandatory skills:
Stripe, , App Development, SaaS Development

jobs.title: HSE (Health Safety Environment) Officer
jobs.description:
As a Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer at REnergy Dynamics Infraworks (REDi), you will play a crucial role in ensuring the implementation of health, safety, and environmental practices at the site of an industrial EPC project. Your responsibilities will include:

- Leading the implementation of site HSE plans and safety procedures
- Conducting daily site safety inspections and toolbox talks
- Monitoring PPE compliance and safe work practices
- Supporting permit-to-work systems and risk assessments
- Reporting and recording near misses, incidents, and unsafe conditions
- Maintaining HSE registers, checklists, and statutory records
- Coordinating with supervisors, subcontractors, and workers on safety matters
- Supporting safety audits, inspections, and compliance reviews

To excel in this role, you are expected to have:
- Total Experience: 35 years
- Prior experience as an HSE Executive / Safety Officer on industrial or infrastructure projects
- Diploma / Degree in Engineering
- NEBOSH / IOSH / ADIS / equivalent safety certification (mandatory)
- Exposure to industrial construction, process plants, or EPC projects preferred

Your work ethic should reflect:
- Belief in safety discipline, teamwork, and accountability
- High integrity, proactive attitude, and reliability
- Readiness to work in challenging site conditions with tight timelines
- Comfort with working alongside large site workforces
- Willingness to adapt to digital tools, mobile apps, and safety reporting systems

Please note that this role requires consistent on-site presence and commitment. Candidates unable to commit full-time to site-based roles are advised not to apply.

If you are an experienced HSE professional with a passion for ensuring a safe work environment and compliance with regulatory standards, we encourage you to apply for the HSE Officer position at REDi. Interested candidates may use the Quick Apply option or share their resume with a brief introduction at +91 82373 23403. jobs.title: Virtual Compliance Officer - Remote Corporate Role
jobs.description:
Job Summary

The Elite Job is seeking a meticulous and experienced Virtual Compliance Officer to join our corporate team remotely. In this critical role, you will be responsible for ensuring that our organization adheres to all relevant regulatory requirements, internal policies, and industry standards. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to implement compliance programs, monitor regulatory changes, conduct risk assessments, and promote a culture of integrity and transparency throughout the company. This role demands strong attention to detail, excellent communication skills, and a proactive approach to managing compliance risks in a virtual environment.
Key Responsibilities
• *

Develop, implement, and maintain compliance programs aligned with regulatory requirements and company policies.
• *

Monitor regulatory changes and assess their impact on company operations.
• *

Conduct regular compliance audits and risk assessments to identify potential gaps and recommend corrective actions.
• *

Provide training and guidance to employees on compliance policies and procedures.
• *

Collaborate with legal, finance, and operations teams to ensure seamless integration of compliance measures.
• *

Prepare and submit compliance reports to senior management and regulatory authorities as required.
• *

Investigate compliance breaches and coordinate remediation efforts promptly.
• *

Maintain accurate documentation of compliance activities and findings.
• *

Serve as the primary point of contact for regulatory agencies and external auditors.
• Required Skills and Qualifications
• *

Bachelors degree in Law, Business Administration, Finance, or a related field.
• *

Professional certifications such as Certified Compliance & Ethics Professional (CCEP) or similar are highly desirable.
• *

Strong knowledge of regulatory frameworks relevant to the industry (e.g., GDPR, SOX, AML, HIPAA).
• *

Proficiency with compliance management software and Microsoft Office Suite.
• *

Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.
• *

Exceptional verbal and written communication skills for clear and effective stakeholder engagement.
• *

Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects in a remote environment.
• Experience
• *

Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in a compliance-related role, preferably in a corporate or regulated environment.
• *

Proven track record of developing and implementing compliance programs and managing regulatory audits.
• *

Experience working remotely or in a virtual corporate setting is an advantage.
• Working Hours
• *

Full-time role with flexible working hours to accommodate different time zones.
• *

Occasional availability for urgent compliance issues or meetings outside of standard business hours may be required.
• Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
• *

In-depth understanding of regulatory requirements and compliance standards within the relevant industry.
• *

Strong ethical judgment and ability to handle sensitive information confidentially.
• *

Ability to interpret complex regulations and translate them into actionable policies.
• *

Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and risk mitigation.
• *

Excellent interpersonal skills to foster collaboration across departments and with external parties.
• Benefits
• *

Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.
• *

Flexible remote working environment promoting work-life balance.
• *

Access to professional development and certification support.
• *

Health and wellness programs tailored for remote employees.
• *

Inclusive and diverse company culture with opportunities for career growth.
• Why Join The Elite Job?

At The Elite Job, we are committed to building a forward-thinking and ethical workplace where compliance is a cornerstone of our success. By joining our team, you will play a pivotal role in shaping our corporate governance framework while enjoying the freedom and flexibility of a remote work setting. We invest in our people, offering continuous learning opportunities and the chance to work alongside industry experts dedicated to excellence and integrity.
How to Apply

Interested candidates are invited to submit their updated resume along with a cover letter highlighting their relevant experience and motivation for the role. Please send your application to us with the subject line Virtual Compliance Officer Application.

We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

jobs.title: US LLC Admin, Compliance Ops, Banking, and Shopify Payments Support
jobs.description:
We are an established multi-country sports company based in Asia, with operations across multiple markets. We are now expanding into the US. Our Colorado LLC is already formed. We do not have a US office yet.

We need a reliable, detail-oriented admin and compliance operations partner to manage ongoing US LLC errands and help us get fully operational with banking and Shopify payouts.
This is not a one-time task. We want long-term support on a monthly retainer.

GOAL

Keep our Colorado LLC compliant and organized, and ensure our US banking and Shopify payouts run smoothly so we can launch and scale in the US.

COMPLIANCE SCOPE
You will help us stay in good standing and keep records organized. You are not acting as our attorney or CPA.

If a task requires legal or tax advice, you will flag it clearly and coordinate with our CPA or legal partner.

SCOPE OF WORK
Phase 1 (Week 1 to 2): Setup and stabilization

1) LLC admin review and document pack
• Review what we already have (Articles or Certificate, EIN if any, Operating Agreement if any, registered agent info)
• Create a clean, organized document pack for banking and platform verification
• Build a simple compliance and admin checklist (what to renew, when, where, and who owns it)
2) Compliance check and good standing setup
• Confirm Colorado Periodic Report status and upcoming deadlines
• Confirm registered agent status and renewal timing
• Confirm entity facts are consistent across documents (legal name, EIN, formation date, addresses)
Mention "Green Strawberry" twice in the 5th line of your proposal
• Confirm whether BOI reporting applies under current rules and document the conclusion
• Create a compliance calendar with links, logins, owners, and renewal dates
3) Address plan (no US office yet)

Recommend a compliant address setup for:
• State filings and registered agent
• IRS mail
• Banking requirements
• Shopify Payments and payout verification
• Customer returns address (coordinate with our operations or fulfillment owner)

Explain pros, cons, risks, and costs of options (registered agent address, virtual mailbox, warehouse or 3PL address, and other valid options).

Important:
We want a fully compliant setup. No fake addresses and no misrepresentation.

4) US business banking
• Help us choose the best bank for our scenario (Mercury preferred, but propose alternatives if better)
• Prepare the application package and guide onboarding steps
• Ensure access and admin setup for our team (users, roles, cards if available, security settings)
• Quick attention check: Mention "Green Strawberry" twice in the 5th line of your proposal.
• Track application status and any followups needed
5) Shopify Payments and payouts
• Guide Shopify Payments activation for our Colorado LLC
• Confirm business details, verification documents, and payout configuration
• Run a test order and test refund (with our Shopify owner) and confirm payouts work
• If Shopify Payments is blocked, propose best alternative gateway options and explain why
Phase 2 (Monthly retainer): Ongoing admin and support
• Track compliance and admin deadlines (Colorado Periodic Report, registered agent renewals, and any state requirements)
• Keep documentation organized and updated when needed
• Support banking admin requests (access changes, documents, troubleshooting)
• Support Shopify payouts troubleshooting and documentation updates
• Coordinate with our CPA and other partners when needed (you are not required to be a CPA or attorney)

DELIVERABLES
- "Current status" summary: what is already done, what is missing, and next steps
• Clean document pack for banking and platform verification
• Entity Facts Sheet (one page summary: legal name, EIN, formation date, registered agent, address types, signer list)
• Compliant address recommendation with cost, risk, and pros and cons summary
• Banking plan and application support (submitted correctly with next steps and a simple status log)
• Shopify Payments and payouts golive checklist and test order and refund confirmation
• Tracking sheet: simple compliance and admin tracker (Google Sheet) with dates, owners, and links
• Monthly retainer plan: price, what is included, and response time expectations
• Shared folder structure with clear naming and an index file:
• 01 Formation
• 02 EIN and IRS
• 03 Registered Agent and Address
• 04 Banking
• 05 Shopify Payments
• 06 Compliance Filings

WHAT WE WILL PROVIDE
• Colorado LLC documents we currently have (and EIN status)
• Owner and passport info if needed for verification
• Any existing registered agent details
• Shopify collaborator access after shortlist
• Fast responses during setup

WHO THIS IS FOR
• You have managed US LLC admin and banking onboarding before (especially with foreign owners or no SSN)
• You understand the difference between registered agent, business address, and mailing address
• You are organized, responsive, and document everything clearly
• You have supported Shopify Payments and payouts setup for US entities, and know common blockers

IMPORTANT NOTES
• We want a fully compliant setup. No fake addresses and no misrepresentation.
• We will not misrepresent our business, address, owners, or operations to banks, Shopify, or any government agency.
• If you cannot handle every part, still apply and clearly list what you can do vs what you would coordinate with a partner.

BUDGET AND TIMELINE
• Start ASAP
• Please propose a fixed price for Phase 1 and a monthly retainer for Phase
• Include estimated hours and milestone breakdown

SCREENING QUESTIONS
1) Have you supported ongoing LLC admin (not just formation) before? What tasks did you handle?

2) Based on a Colorado LLC with no US office, what address setup do you recommend for banking and Shopify verification, and why?

3) Have you helped foreign owners open a US business bank account? Which banks worked best and what were the blockers?

4) Have you set up Shopify Payments and payouts for a US LLC before? What are the most common verification issues?

5)

Pricing:
Phase 1 fixed price and estimated hours, and monthly retainer price with included scope and response time.

Contract duration of more than 6 months. with 40 hours per week.

Mandatory skills:
Administrative Support, Legal Applications & Registration, Legal Documentation, Legal Calendaring, File Documentation

jobs.title: Compliance AND Safety Officer - Regulatory AND Marine Standards
jobs.description:
Job Title: Compliance & Safety Officer – Regulatory & Marine Standards
Location: Onsite / Hybrid / Remote (depending on organization)
Job Type: Full-Time
Department: Compliance / Health & Safety / Marine Operations
Experience Level: Mid-Level to Senior
Industry: Maritime / Logistics / Offshore Operations / Safety & Compliance

We are seeking a Compliance & Safety Officer with strong expertise in regulatory compliance and marine safety standards to join our operational team. This role is critical in ensuring our maritime and logistics operations align with both local and international laws, including safety protocols, environmental regulations, and industry best practices.
Key Responsibilities
• *

Ensure compliance with international maritime regulations (e.g., IMO, SOLAS, MARPOL, ISM Code) and national safety laws
• *

Conduct safety inspections and audits of vessels, offshore facilities, and operations
• *

Oversee risk assessments and incident investigations to identify and mitigate hazards
• *

Develop and implement safety training programs and standard operating procedures (SOPs)
• *

Monitor changes in laws, standards, and policies affecting marine operations
• *

Prepare reports and maintain documentation for regulatory bodies and internal audits
• *

Liaise with port authorities, classification societies, and external auditors during compliance checks
• *

Lead or support emergency response planning and drills
• Required Qualifications
• *

Bachelor degree in Marine Engineering, Occupational Safety, Environmental Science, or related field
• *

In-depth knowledge of marine regulatory frameworks and health & safety standards
• *

Certifications such as NEBOSH, IOSH, ISM Lead Auditor, or similar are highly preferred
• *

Minimum 3–5 years of experience in maritime safety, compliance, or HSE roles
• *

Familiarity with vessel safety inspections, risk assessment methods, and accident investigation procedures
• *

Strong analytical and documentation skills with attention to detail
• *

Proficiency in MS Office and safety management systems (SMS)
• Preferred Qualifications
• *

Prior experience with offshore platforms, shipping companies, or port operations
• *

Understanding of environmental regulations and sustainability practices in marine contexts
• *

Strong interpersonal skills for cross-department coordination and crew engagement
• *

Willingness to travel to ports, vessels, or offshore sites as needed
• Work Environment & Conditions
• *

Office-based, hybrid, or remote with periodic field visits depending on the role
• *

May involve travel to international maritime locations, dockyards, or vessels
• *

Flexible working hours when overseeing global operations or audits
• Benefits
• *

Competitive salary based on experience and qualifications
• *

Travel and accommodation expenses covered for site visits
• *

Health and life insurance packages
• *

Professional development and certifications support
• *

Career progression opportunities within safety, operations, or regulatory affairs
• How to Apply

Please submit the following:
• *

An updated resume/CV highlighting marine or compliance experience
• *

A cover letter explaining your motivation and relevant qualifications
• *

Any professional certifications or licenses related to marine compliance or safety
• Subject Line: Application – Compliance & Safety Officer (Marine Standards)

This role is ideal for a detail-oriented professional with a passion for safety, regulatory excellence, and operational integrity in the maritime industry. Join a team that values proactive compliance and strives to uphold the highest safety benchmarks on the seas.

MVN Engineering Services provides Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) design and consultancy services to architects and MEP consultants throughout the United States. With expertise spanning mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and fire protection systems, MVN delivers comprehensive engineering solutions for both commercial and residential buildings. jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer - Robotic Enclosure & Harnessing Design | Apprentice
jobs.description:
As a Mechanical Engineer – Robotic Enclosure & Harnessing Design Apprentice at Origin (formerly 10xConstruction) , you will be responsible for designing the “body” of the robot.
You will protect the robot’s electronics by designing a high-performance, aesthetically-conscious enclosure system that can withstand harsh construction environments.
This role sits at the intersection of industrial design, thermal management, and electromechanical integration, offering a unique opportunity to contribute to a revolutionary product from the ground up.
Key Responsibilities - Robotic Enclosure Design Design robust enclosures and control panels for electronic components, ensuring serviceability and ease of access.
Lead mechanical design for Ingress Protection (IP rating) against dust, water, and debris.
Design vibration mounting and isolation systems to protect sensitive electronics.
Design for mobile robot requirements including sensor mounting, EMI/EMC shielding, and thermal management.
Thermal Management Conduct thermal analysis (CFD) and design active/passive cooling solutions (heat sinks, fans, etc.
) to ensure component performance in extreme temperatures.
Wire Harness & Cable Management (Key Deliverable) Design and route major wire harnesses and cable management systems for reliability and serviceability.
Plan integration of all electrical subsystems including: High-power systems (actuators).
Logic/network systems (compute).
Sensitive systems (sensors).
Create detailed harness documentation including routing diagrams and manufacturing specifications.
Ensure grounding, earthing, and EMI/EMC shielding strategies are incorporated into mechanical design.
Systems Engineering & Verification & Validation (V&V) Develop engineering requirements and V&V protocols for thermal performance, ingress protection, shock/vibration, and EMI/EMC.
Conduct DFMEA and risk analysis for enclosure and harness designs.
Maintain documentation related to enclosure compliance and testing.
Prototyping & Testing Lead rapid prototyping of enclosures using 3D printing, sheet metal fabrication, and other methods.
Conduct testing such as thermal tests, water ingress tests, and drop tests to validate designs.
Why Join Origin?
Work in a dynamic start-up environment at the forefront of robotics innovation.
Contribute to technology that will transform the construction industry.
Work alongside a passionate team committed to real-world impact.
Get hands-on ownership and build systems that will ship into real environments.
Graduates only.
Relevant work experience: >6 months.
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, or related field.
Strong proficiency in CAD software (SolidWorks, etc.
) and GD&T.
Experience/exposure to enclosure design for IP ratings, thermal management (CFD), and vibration.
Exposure to designing and documenting wire harnesses and cable management solutions.
Familiarity with manufacturing processes such as sheet metal, injection molding, and 3D printing.
Strong communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
Preferred Qualifications Design portfolio showcasing relevant projects.
Experience with regulatory compliance for enclosures (NEMA, UL, etc.
). Hiring Note This is an Apprentice position.
We are looking for Immediate Joiners (or candidates with minimal notice period).

jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer for Non-Electric Shower Water Limiter
jobs.description:
Hi,

I am developing a non-electric water-saving shower product and I need an experienced mechanical/product design engineer to design the core limiter module.
Project Goal

Design a fully mechanical shower water limiter cartridge that allows the user to set a water budget per shower.

Key Features Required

Selector dial: 5L to 50L + ∞ (Unlimited mode)

Mechanical volume measurement (turbine + gears or equivalent)

Reset mechanism

Automatic trickle mode when the limit is reached (not full shutoff)

Integrated flow stabilizer upstream of the meter for accuracy at varying pressures

Standard shower connection:
G1/2 thread

Compact cartridge design suitable for integration into a shower rail system

Deliverables

Mechanism feasibility concept

3D CAD model (SolidWorks preferred)

STEP file for manufacturing

Basic technical drawings

Simple BOM (bill of materials)

Important

This must be a fully mechanical solution (no electronics, no sensors, no batteries).

Ideal Experience

Please apply only if you have experience designing:
Valves, flow meters, or plumbing cartridge mechanisms

Consumer plumbing products (showers, faucets, water-saving devices)

Manufacturable mechanical mechanisms (DFM)

To Apply

Please answer these 2 questions in your proposal:
Have you designed any valves, flow meters, or plumbing cartridges before? Please share examples or CAD screenshots.

What is your estimated fixed-price cost in AED for the full feasibility + CAD package?

Thank you.

Contract duration of less than 1 month. with 30 hours per week.

Mandatory skills:
CAD, SolidWorks, Mechanical Engineering, Mechanical Design, Product Design, Engineering Design

Job Summary

HR Service Jobs is seeking enthusiastic and motivated Trainee Engineers (Mechanical and Civil) to join our dynamic team. This is an exciting opportunity for fresh graduates who are eager to launch their careers in engineering. • * Support the preparation of project documentation, including reports, specifications, and technical drawings.
• * Conduct research and gather data to inform engineering decisions.

Multi-Source Scraper:
Connect n8n to scraping tools to monitor BizBuySell, , LoopNet, and Flippa.

Notion Command Center:

Human-in-the-Loop Workflow: A trigger in Notion that executes a personalized outreach sequence once a deal is marked "Approved."

How would you handle de-duplication if the same business is listed on three different websites?
